findings.feature: Overall frame W:H proportion (600 x 1991 mm)
findings.evidence: Render frame: outer width 285-291 px, height 760 px (y=10 to 770) -> W:H = 0.379. Real AR3140: 600/1991 = 0.301. The rail hole pattern internally proves the scale: 1U = 16.84 px -> 0.379 px/mm, which makes the render height 2005 mm (correct) but the outer width 285/0.379 = 752-768 mm. The frame is modeled ~27% too wide; a flat front face under rectilinear projection preserves W:H, so this is model geometry, not camera.
findings.fix_hint: Keep the rails exactly where they are (their 465 mm c-c is correct) and pull each side wall + corner post inboard by ~80 mm so outer width = 600 mm. Do NOT scale the whole frame horizontally or you will break the currently-correct EIA rail spacing.

findings.feature: Front mounting-rail c-c spacing (EIA-310, 465.1 mm)
findings.evidence: Left rail hole strip centered x=191.5, right x=367.5 -> 176 px apart. At the rail-derived scale (0.379 px/mm) that is 464.5 mm vs 465.1 mm EIA spec — within 1 px. Midpoint 279.5 matches frame centerline exactly.
findings.fix_hint: None. Freeze rail x-positions during the width fix.

findings.feature: Square-hole 3-per-U rhythm and pitch
findings.evidence: Left rail pitch sequence groups cleanly into period-3: (5, 6, 6) px repeating, ratio 0.83 vs EIA 12.7:15.875 = 0.80; per-U sums 16.8-17.5 px, mean 16.84 px = 44.4 mm (spec 44.45). Hole zone spans 707 px = 42.0 U. 116 holes resolved vs 126 nominal — the deficit is quantization merging of 3 px holes, not a modeling error, since per-U sums stay at 17 px throughout.
findings.fix_hint: None on pattern. Holes measure ~3 x 3.5 px = 8-9 mm vs 9.5 mm spec — at the limit of resolution; verify hole size is 9.5 mm square in the source geometry before the next higher-res pass.

findings.feature: Leveling feet / casters / floor gap under base
findings.evidence: Reference gallery_10 shows the base frame raised off the floor on visible silver leveling feet (clear light gap under the bottom frame member, feet at all 4 corners, casters inboard). Render: plinth face (y=720-770) runs straight into its contact shadow, posts reach y=768-770, zero floor gap, zero feet. Every real cabinet front reads 'standing on feet'; the render reads 'extruded from the floor'.
findings.fix_hint: Add 4 leveling feet: ~M12 threaded stem, pad diameter 40-50 mm, set at the corner-post footprints, lifting the frame 25-40 mm off the floor; optionally add the 4 casters inboard of the feet (visible in the ref's under-base shadow line).

findings.feature: Corner post front-face width
findings.evidence: Render post faces are 11 px = 29 mm at model scale (0.038 of outer width). NetShelter-class front vertical frame faces read ~45 mm (~0.075 of the 600 mm width in gallery_10/11). Render posts are barely more than half the correct visual weight, and this will worsen after the width fix unless corrected (11 px on a 227 px-wide frame = 0.048).
findings.fix_hint: Widen each front corner-post face to 45 mm (17 px at current scale). Post depth should return toward the door plane; keep the post inner edge clear of the 465 mm rail line (rail centerline sits 67.5 mm from the outer edge on the real 600 mm cabinet).

findings.feature: Top front beam (roof edge band) depth
findings.evidence: Render top front member is y=10-19 -> 10 px = 26 mm tall, uniform across the width. In gallery_10 the roof edge + top frame band reads ~40-50 mm (roughly 2x the render), giving the cabinet its capped, heavy-browed top. Render top reads like a picture frame, not a roof.
findings.fix_hint: Deepen the front top horizontal band to ~45 mm (17 px). The rails should still start immediately below it (they currently start at y=20, which is correct behavior).

findings.feature: Rear vertical mounting rails (second holed pair)
findings.evidence: Reference cabinets have 4 mounting rails; in a straight-front view the rear pair is visible slightly inboard of the front pair with the same square-hole pattern (gallery_10 shows both pairs). Render shows one plain mid-gray vertical bar per side (left bar x=166-185, starting only at y=76, depth-consistent with a rear member) with no hole pattern whatsoever, and no symmetric holed rear pair is detectable anywhere in the interior.
findings.fix_hint: Model the rear rail pair as duplicates of the front rails (same 465.1 mm c-c, same 42U hole pattern), placed at the rear rail depth (~800-900 mm behind the front plane for a 1070 mm-deep AR3140). If the plain bars are meant to BE those rails, add the hole pattern to them.

findings.feature: Door hinge bosses / latch keeper on front posts
findings.evidence: Gallery_10/11 show hinge blocks on the door-side front frame (two-plus hinge bosses protruding from the post line) and a latch keeper on the opposite post. Render posts are featureless rectangular extrusions end to end — no cutouts, no bosses, no fastener detail at any scan line.
findings.fix_hint: Add 2-3 hinge bosses (~60 mm tall, protruding ~15 mm proud of the post face) spaced along one front post, and a latch keeper plate at mid-height on the other; even at this render scale they break the dead-straight post silhouette the way the ref does.

findings.feature: Rail U-number markings and rail-vs-frame tonal differentiation
findings.evidence: Gallery_10 rails carry a white/light U-numbering scale strip every U — it is the strongest visual identifier of a mounting rail in the ref. Render rails are the same uniform gray as the frame with no scale strip. Note: on the AR3140 the rails themselves are black like the frame; the zinc tone belongs to cage-nut hardware (see cagenut macro), NOT the rail. Do not fix this by making rails zinc.
findings.fix_hint: Add the U-number scale strip geometry: a ~6 mm-wide printed/embossed band on the rail flange adjacent to the hole column, one number + tick per 44.45 mm. Keep rail base material matched to frame; reserve zinc for hardware.

findings.feature: Base plinth (front valance) height
findings.evidence: Render plinth face y=720-770 -> 50 px = 132 mm at model scale; the ref's lower front frame/valance band reads ~100-130 mm. Within tolerance at this resolution.
findings.fix_hint: None on height; it just needs to float on the feet per the feet finding instead of touching the floor.

findings.feature: Roof cable cutouts, side-panel split seam and latches
findings.evidence: Both are prominent in the refs (roof brush-strip cutouts in gallery_10 top; split side panels with two quarter-turn locks) but are geometrically invisible from a dead-front camera. The render shows no top surface (top band is 10 px flat) and sides edge-on.
findings.fix_hint: Cannot audit from this frame. Add a 3/4 or top-front render angle to the proof set before claiming roof/side geometry parity.

grades.why: Height and everything vertical is right (42U zone = 707 px, total height back-computes to 2005 mm vs 1991 real), but the outer width is ~760 mm vs 600 mm — a 27% error on the single most defining proportion. W:H reads 0.379 vs 0.301; the cabinet looks squat at a glance.

Genuinely excellent: correct 3-hole-per-U rhythm with the 12.7/15.875/15.875 short-long-long pattern measurably present (5,6,6 px groups), 1U = 44.4 mm, 42.0 U total span, rail c-c = 464.5 mm vs 465.1 spec. Docked 2 points: hole size reads 8-9 mm vs 9.5 (unconfirmable at 3 px), no chamfer/countersink detail, no U-markings.

Posts are featureless rectangular extrusions at ~29 mm face width vs ~45 mm real — wrong weight, no hinge bosses, no latch keeper, no fastener detail, and their position embodies the 80 mm-per-side width error. Only the full-height run and clean verticality are right.

The render is a clean-room abstraction: no leveling feet or floor gap (the single most visible omission on every real cabinet front), no hinge/latch hardware, no rear holed rail pair, no U-number strips, no rail/frame differentiation. Base plinth height is the main survivor.

A machinist would reject it in two seconds on width and the floor-contact base, but the underlying rack science — EIA rail spacing, hole rhythm, U count, height — is verifiably correct, so the fix path is narrow and mechanical: pull the sides in 80 mm each, add feet, thicken posts and top band, add hardware details.

notes: Reference-quality caveats: (1) ar3140_gallery_1.jpg is a vertical cable-manager accessory (finger-style organizer), not the enclosure — it contributed nothing to the frame audit; the enclosure ground truth is gallery_10 and gallery_11 only. (2) Both enclosure refs are 3/4 views with the perforated door open — there is no straight-front bare-frame reference photo, so real post-face and top-band widths are estimated from the 3/4 views plus published NetShelter SX dimensions (600 x 1070 x 1991 mm, 42U, EIA-310 465.1 mm), not measured square-on. (3) The cage-nut macro pins the 9.5 mm square-hole target and confirms zinc belongs to hardware, not rails. (4) Render camera is a hair above mid-height (outer edges converge ~5 px top-to-bottom) — negligible for all measurements; I used the rail's internally-proven scale (1U = 16.84 px -> 0.379 px/mm) as the ruler for every mm figure, which is what exposes the width error unambiguously. (5) The 116-vs-126 hole count is quantization (3 px holes merging), not a geometry defect — per-U sums hold at 17 px across the full span. Missing angles for a complete audit: top-front 3/4 (roof cutouts), side elevation (panel split seam, caster/feet layout), rear (rear rail detail).

findings.feature: mesh lattice constants (triangle punch pattern)
findings.evidence: Queue/MEASUREMENTS spec P=2.87 R=2.59 (claimed 10px fundamental at 3.480px/mm). Re-measured on full-res front_A three ways: autocorrelation of patch x250-550/y700-1000 gives horizontal fundamental 16px; hole-centroid nearest-neighbor median 16.75px (417 components); visual count in 6x zoom ~17.5px. Vertical 2-row repeat 26.5px -> row pitch 13.25px. At 3.457px/mm: same-orientation period_H = 4.85mm, row pitch = 3.83mm, up/down alternation offset = 2.4mm. Their 2.87/2.59 is 41%/32% small — 2.6x too many holes per area
findings.fix_hint: Rebuild the cell: equilateral triangles alternating up/down, open-triangle side 3.6±0.3mm, bar (web) width 0.7-0.9mm, corner radius ~0.4mm, horizontal same-orientation period 4.85mm, row pitch 3.83mm (2-row vertical repeat 7.66mm), skin thickness 1.0-1.2mm, open fraction ~0.5. ALSO fix acceptance (c) in RACK-LOOP.md — it locks the render to 2.87/2.59 ±5%, so the loop would pass wrong geometry; re-run the scratchpad FFT on the full-res 1600px front_A first to arbitrate (their crop was likely downscaled ~0.6x)

findings.feature: fine perforated filter layer behind the punched door skin
findings.evidence: Every large triangle in front_A shows a fine dot texture inside it (6x zoom: dot pitch ~4.3px = 1.25mm, dot dia ~2.5px = 0.7mm). This fine-dot read is THE visible depth texture in bright front light — the fans/hex wall do not read at all. The queue's interior stack is door -> 20mm gap -> fan discs, with no filter layer
findings.fix_hint: Add a fine perforated sheet 1-2mm behind the door skin: round holes dia ~0.7mm, staggered pitch ~1.25mm, spanning the full door. Model as real holes or high-res opacity map; it must read inside every big triangle at portrait distance

findings.feature: center lock — assembly size and missing bail handle
findings.evidence: Queue: 'lock cylinder ~9mm dia'. Measured in front_A (4x crop): raised bezel ring OD 67px = 19.5mm, ring section ~2.5mm, inner barrel face ~12-14mm, keyway vertical slot 2.2mm wide x 6.5mm tall with flared top. A vertical wing/bail turn-handle wraps the bezel: total span 124px = 35.8mm, arm width ~5.5mm, proud ~4-6mm, resting ~10 deg off vertical — the bail is the most visible element of the lock and is not in the plan at all
findings.fix_hint: Model: bezel torus OD 19.5mm/section 2.5mm; barrel face dia 13mm recessed ~1mm inside ring; keyway 2.2x6.5mm vertical slot with rounded flare at top; bail handle = flat stirrup, arm 5.5mm wide x ~2.5mm thick, spanning 36mm vertically over the bezel with a short flattened grip tab top and bottom, rotated ~10 deg

findings.feature: lock vertical position
findings.evidence: Queue: '~14mm below face top' (self-flagged APPROX, derived from a contaminated 37-23 subtraction in MEASUREMENTS.md). Measured lock center: src y=658 -> 81px = 23.4mm below the door top edge (y=577), = ~35.5mm below the front-face top (y~535), = 58mm below silhouette top. Horizontal: center x=799 vs face center 798.5 — dead center
findings.fix_hint: Place lock center at face-center-x, 23.5mm below the DOOR top edge (equivalently 35.5mm below the front-face top; door top edge is 12mm below face top: 10mm lid fascia + 2mm seam groove). Lock sits at 15% of the 155mm door height

findings.feature: badge zone — actual shape is a proud keystone plate with chevron bottom, not a generic recess
findings.evidence: Queue says only 'recessed badge zone'. front_A + q34: the badge zone is an UNPERFORATED continuation of the door skin (flush, not recessed) shaped like a keystone/shield: top edge merges with the door top border, width 432px = 125mm (0.28 of 440mm body, 0.26 of 482.6mm), sides step inward at ~42 deg, bottom edge is a wide shallow chevron whose center vertex sits ~60mm below the door top edge (mesh resumes at src y~775-790). Logo (trademark-gated, shape only): snowflake ~13mm + text band ~8mm tall, centered on x, band center ~46mm below door top
findings.fix_hint: Cut the plate as an unperforated keystone in the door skin: 125mm top width along the door top border, 42 deg inward side slopes for ~25-30mm drop, then shallow chevron (~12 deg per side) meeting a center vertex 60mm below door top. Deboss only the logo lockup zone ~0.15mm. All plate boundaries must be stepped with 2-3 rows of shrinking fade triangles, not clean cuts

findings.feature: mesh border edge treatment (fade rows)
findings.evidence: At every door border and along the badge plate edges, the lattice does not stop abruptly: 2-3 rows/columns of progressively smaller triangles (roughly 60%/30% linear scale) transition into solid margin. Solid margins measure: left/right ~20px = 6mm, top ~6mm, bottom ~3mm (tighter). Queue models a plain '~8mm border frame' with no fade
findings.fix_hint: Solid margin 6mm sides/top and 3mm bottom, then 2 transition rows of shrunken triangles (approx 60% then 30% of full size, same lattice registration) before full-size lattice. Same treatment along the badge plate's sloped and chevron edges. Without this the door reads die-cut CAD-fake at portrait distance

findings.feature: interior depth stack order (hex fan wall omitted)
findings.evidence: Queue: '3x 120mm fan discs+hubs ~20mm behind the door'. fan_detail + io_macro: fans mount BEHIND a hex-perforated steel wall (hex across-flats ~6.5mm, pitch ~8.5mm, web ~1.9mm — cross-checked between both macros), which itself sits ~15-25mm behind the door; wall also carries obround punch-outs (~32mm long, pressed flange) and rows of screw holes for alternate fan positions. Front-on in bright light none of this reads (their own L~16 finding); it emerges at raking/close angles
findings.fix_hint: Stack: door skin (1.2mm) -> fine filter (+1-2mm) -> cavity -> hex-perf wall at 15-25mm (AF 6.5mm, pitch 8.5mm) -> fan frames clamped to its rear face, blades another ~5mm back. Fans must never cross the wall plane, let alone the door plane

findings.feature: front fan count
findings.evidence: Queue plans 3 fans; rm44_fan_detail shows exactly 2 installed, side-by-side nearly touching, with empty hex wall and spare mount holes beside them. 7 wide-chord blades each, blank hubs (no sticker) with 4 spot marks, hub dia ~0.35 of frame. Fan size (80 vs 120mm) is indeterminate from the macro — no absolute scale
findings.fix_hint: Model 2 fans (photo truth) mounted adjacent behind the hex wall, or produce a vendor spec citation for 3 before spending the third instance. Hubs blank, 7 blades, hub/frame ratio 0.35

findings.feature: top lid seam / front fascia dimensions
findings.evidence: Queue item 'top lid seam + bottom rail seam' exists but carries no numbers. Measured front_A: lid front fascia band ~10mm tall (rows 535-570), then a ~2mm deep shadow groove (570-577), then door top edge with small chamfer highlight. q34 confirms the lid slightly overhangs and a faint transverse seam crosses the top surface ~15% back from the front edge (front strip fixed, main cover separate — low confidence, 640px ref)
findings.fix_hint: Fascia 10mm, groove 2mm, door top edge chamfer ~0.5mm. Optionally add the transverse top-surface seam ~70mm back from the front edge at low relief

findings.feature: bottom rail seam and door-proud step
findings.evidence: Planned generically; measured: door bottom edge at y=1114 with a thin bright chamfer line, bottom rail band 33px = 9.5mm tall, recessed behind the door face; q34/b_front show the door standing proud of the surrounding frame by ~5-8mm with a shadow gap; one tiny fastener/notch on the rail top edge near the right end (macro-only)
findings.fix_hint: Door proud of frame by 6mm; rail 9.5mm tall set back; 1-1.5mm shadow gap between door bottom and rail; door height 155mm of the 177mm face (155.3mm measured)

findings.feature: rack ears + thumbscrews (queue item 'ears to 482.6mm')
findings.evidence: NONE of the 5 references shows an ear: front_A, q34 and bottom_feet are all bare-chassis product shots (door spans the full 440mm body, no flange visible). io_macro hints at the eared config — a vertical rod handle (~12mm dia, curved ends, Phillips-mounted) at the front-left that only exists with ears/handles installed. Ear fold radius, thumbscrew count/positions/knurl cannot be cataloged from this set
findings.fix_hint: Acquire an ear reference (SilverStone RM44 manual drawing or an eared photo) before modeling queue item 1; decide bare vs eared+handles config — a racked unit needs ears, so this is a real gap, not optional

findings.feature: door corner deboss circles (latch/magnet marks)
findings.evidence: Both bottom corners of the door carry two faint debossed circle rings: dia ~25px = 7.2mm, vertical spacing ~14mm c-c, centers ~11-12mm inboard of the door side edge; lower circle ~11mm above the door bottom edge, upper ~25mm. Confirmed left (blcorner) and right (brcorner) crops. Not in the queue
findings.fix_hint: Add 2 debossed rings per bottom corner, 7mm dia, ~0.2mm deep, at the positions above, mirrored left/right

findings.feature: front I/O pod behind the door (left side)
findings.evidence: io_macro: vertical rounded-rect pod ~24mm wide (scaled via USB-C opening = 8.9mm, USB-A = 13mm) mounted proud on the hex wall via keyhole slots at the chassis front-left: power button 11mm sq with pill window, reset 4mm sq, 3 round LEDs ~3mm (HDD + LAN1 + LAN2 icons), USB-C, 2x USB-A stacked. Reads through the mesh only at close/angled views; invisible in the bright dead-front shot
findings.fix_hint: Low-poly pod at front-left interior, 24mm wide, faces forward ~5mm behind the filter plane; needed only for raking/macro rungs of the detail ladder and the LED variance system (the power LED that RACK state machine toggles lives here)

findings.feature: interior darkness gradient (what reads through the mesh)
findings.evidence: front_A: mesh+interior reads near-uniform L~16, but a broad V-shaped DARKER zone extends below the badge plate (apex down, reaching ~135mm below the door top at center x), while the outer thirds read ~2-3 L brighter where the filter/hex wall sit closer. No fan rings or hubs are discernible front-on in studio light. Queue's interior item models geometry but no plan encodes this gradient acceptance
findings.fix_hint: With the corrected 4-layer stack the gradient should emerge physically; verify by patch-sampling the render front-on: center-below-badge patch must be the darkest, outer thirds +2-3 L, no visible fan ring signature at portrait distance

findings.feature: side wrap / visible fasteners at the front edge
findings.evidence: q_side/b_side: side panel is a flat sheet with a vertical column of 6-8 rivets/screws on the front flange just behind the door side face, plus scattered countersunk option holes mid-panel; top lid edge overhangs the side with a seam line. When racked, only the first ~10-20mm of side (the rivet column zone) could catch light past the ears. Queue's front-facing doctrine covers this but no side detail is planned
findings.fix_hint: Add just the front flange strip: door side face (1.2mm skin return), 2-3mm gap, frame post with 6 rivet dome heads ~3mm dia at ~22mm vertical spacing. Skip the mid-panel holes per front-facing doctrine

findings.feature: chassis body proportions 440 x 176
findings.evidence: Grounded spec 176 x 440 x 468 checks against front_A: full face height (front-face top y~535 to bottom y=1147) = 612px = 177mm vs 176 spec (+0.6%); width anchor by definition. MEASUREMENTS' face_height_read autopsy (199.2mm contamination by the foreshortened lid plane) is correctly diagnosed — silhouette top y=457 vs face top y~535
findings.fix_hint: Keep 440 x 176 x 468; body reveal 1.8mm per 4U slot as already grounded. When compositing vs the 482.6mm ear width, all my mm figures scale by 0.912

findings.feature: feet (bottom ref)
findings.evidence: rm44_bottom_feet.jpg is NOT a bottom view — it is a second elevated 3/4 (640x480) showing top, front and right side; no bottom face or feet visible in any reference. Racked front-on, feet would be invisible regardless
findings.fix_hint: Do not model feet; rename or replace the mislabeled reference so a later loop doesn't hallucinate feet from the filename

grades.why: The face itself is richly covered: a 1600px dead-front hero (driver-inspected), two 3/4s, and two interior macros whose hex-wall readings cross-check (6.5mm AF in both). But the set has holes exactly where the queue needs it: zero ear/thumbscrew coverage despite ears being queue item 1, the 'bottom_feet' ref is mislabeled (no bottom/feet in the set), no raking-light mesh macro to pin web profile and skin thickness, no absolute scale in the fan macro (80 vs 120mm fans indeterminate), and both 3/4s are only 640x480

All 5 queue items (body+ears, mesh, lock, seams, interior fans) exist, and body dims + seam items are sound. But 3 of 5 carry numbers that contradict the reference: mesh lattice constants off 41%/32% (and baked into acceptance (c), so the loop would self-certify wrong geometry), lock diameter ~2x small with the dominant bail handle unplanned, lock z-position 2.5x high, fan count 3 vs photographed 2, and the interior stack omits the hex wall the fans actually mount to. Ears are planned but have no reference grounding at all

L0 (body/proportions) and L1 (mesh field, seams) rungs are planned. The texture-defining L2 rungs are absent: the fine filter dots that are the ONLY visible depth texture front-on, the border fade-triangle rows, the keystone badge plate silhouette, the lock bail. L3 macro rungs (corner deboss rings, I/O pod, hex wall obrounds, rail fastener) are also absent. As planned, the door would read as a clean CAD punch-out — geometrically countable as wrong within seconds at portrait distance

notes: Scale basis: front_A body span cols 38-1559 = 1521px = 440mm -> 3.457 px/mm (MEASUREMENTS.md used 3.480, 0.7% agreement; all mm figures ±3% incl. perspective). Ratios vs the 482.6mm ear width = my numbers x 0.912 (e.g. badge plate 125mm = 0.259, lock ring 19.5mm = 0.040, door height 155mm = 0.321 of ear width). Vertical map (front_A px): silhouette top 457 / face top ~535 / lid groove 570-577 / door 577-1114 / rail 1114-1147. Angles we lack: true side elevation, rear (out of scope per front-facing doctrine), raking front macro of the mesh, any ear/thumbscrew view, true bottom. Reference conflicts to log: (1) rm44_bottom_feet.jpg is a mislabeled 3/4, (2) fan_detail shows a zinc/galvanized silver interior while the through-mesh read is near-black — keep low interior albedo for the through-door read but expect silver to appear if a door-open or deep-raking shot is ever framed, (3) io_macro shows the eared/handled config (vertical rod handle ~12mm dia) while all three exterior shots are bare-chassis — pick one config before modeling. Trademark gate respected: logo cataloged as deboss zone dimensions only (snowflake ~13mm, text band ~8mm tall, band center ~46mm below door top). Highest-leverage single fix before any modeling: arbitrate the mesh lattice constants (my 4.85/3.83mm vs the ledger's 2.87/2.59mm) by re-running the FFT script on the full-res hero — acceptance criterion (c) inherits whichever number wins, so this decides whether the whole loop converges to the right door.

findings.feature: Chassis primary massing + faceplate seam
findings.evidence: CAD: 443mm W x 44.3mm H (1U) x 297mm D, face H:W = 0.100. Front photo: lid overhangs the face; horizontal seam where lid front edge meets faceplate top runs full width; lid front fold radius ~1mm. Vertical face corners wrap to sides with r~1-2mm. No bezel frame — face is one flat plate wall-to-wall.
findings.fix_hint: Model body 443x44.3x297mm; separate faceplate plane inset under a lid lip; put the lid-to-face seam line at the very top of the face; corner radii 1-2mm.

findings.feature: 48-port RJ45 grid GROUP structure (4 gangs of 2x6)
findings.evidence: Photo top-row apertures: 24 columns in 4 groups of 6; group column spans x25-167, 177-319, 330-472, 483-626 (each 142-143px = 82.5mm = 0.187 of body W); inter-gang divider 10px = 5.8mm (0.013 W); gang start-to-start pitch 152.7px = 88.4mm. Field spans 6.7mm to 357mm from left face edge (0.79 W).
findings.fix_hint: Instance a 12-port GANG (2 rows x 6 cols, 83mm wide), NOT a bare cavity x48: 4 gangs at 88.4mm pitch starting 6.7mm from left face edge, separated by 5.9mm dividers (tan gang side-walls + dark 1mm center seam, NOT white face metal).

findings.feature: RJ45 port pitch and aperture proportions
findings.evidence: Photo: within-gang pitch 24px = 13.9mm (CAD cross-check 13.5mm; true ~14.0 = 0.0316 W). Aperture 22px wide = ~12.4mm; row cavity height ~10.5mm (bottom row 18px/1.648); inter-column webs 2-3px = 1.5mm tan plastic.
findings.fix_hint: Port aperture 12.4 x 10.5mm at 14.0mm pitch; 1.5mm tan webs between columns; 24 columns total.

findings.feature: SFP+ 2x2 cage cluster
findings.evidence: Photo: top cages at x637-661 and 663-687 → aperture 24-25px = 14.2mm wide, cage pitch 26px = 15.0mm; cluster window ~361-394mm from left edge (center at 0.85 W); 2 rows matching RJ45 row heights (~10mm each); bottom pair merges optically (empty open cages, EMI finger texture inside). CAD confirms one shared window for all 4.
findings.fix_hint: One cutout ~33 x 25mm at 361mm; two 14.2mm cages per row at 15.0mm pitch, 2 rows; open dark interiors with spring-finger relief.

findings.feature: QSFP+ 2x1 stacked cage pair
findings.evidence: Photo: aperture x695-728 = 33-34px = 19.4mm wide (vs 14.2 SFP — clearly wider); window ~396-416mm from left edge; two cages stacked with a horizontal latch band between. CAD draws top cage with 4 dashed louver slots, bottom cage with white pull-tab.
findings.fix_hint: Separate cage type: 19.4mm-wide apertures, single column, 2 stacked, window at 396-416mm; do NOT reuse SFP cage width.

findings.feature: Mirrored bottom row (latch + LED orientation flip)
findings.evidence: Photo macro + CAD gang drawing agree: top-row jacks have latch notch UP (center-top, ~4mm wide) with LED pair at TOP corners; bottom-row jacks are vertically mirrored — notch DOWN, LED pair at BOTTOM corners. Rows share a 7px = 4mm horizontal tan web; columns vertically aligned (photo offsets ±3px are lens parallax).
findings.fix_hint: Instance the cavity with a Z-mirror for the bottom row; keep columns aligned; 4mm web between rows.

findings.feature: Recessed gang window + tan monoblock bezel
findings.evidence: Photo: each gang is one tan/beige plastic monoblock (lum ~100-130 vs face 220) sitting ~1-2mm behind the faceplate; thin dark shadow seam at window top; white faceplate return edge ~1px visible at window sides. Window height ~44px = 25-27mm.
findings.fix_hint: Cut a 83 x 25mm window per gang, recess the tan connector block 1.5mm behind face; gloss plastic (semi-gloss) bezel, chamfered aperture edges r~0.5mm.

findings.feature: Console + MGMT RJ45 stack (far right)
findings.evidence: Photo: 2x1 stacked RJ45 at x736-759 (window 420.5-434mm from left edge), same gang construction as grid; TOP jack has NO LED windows (console), BOTTOM jack has lit LED pair at bottom corners (ethernet). Own cutout with rounded silkscreen bracket above (label ~7 glyphs) and short label below.
findings.fix_hint: Add a 13.5 x 25mm single-column RJ45 stack at 420.5mm; reuse mirrored-pair cavity but delete LED windows on the top jack.

findings.feature: Rack ears (RM)
findings.evidence: CGI: L-bracket ears; front flange full 1U height, extends ~15-18mm beyond body side, 2 ROUND holes ~O6mm at ~22% and ~78% of height, outer corners chamfered ~45deg; side plate runs ~60-80mm back along chassis with a diagonal rear taper (CAD side view) and 2 countersunk screws. STH front photo shows the device with ears REMOVED — body ends are plain.
findings.fix_hint: Model ears as separate bolt-on L-brackets: flange 44 x 16mm, 2 round O6 holes, chamfered corners, tapered side plate ~70mm deep; flange plane approximately flush with face (0 to +3mm).

findings.feature: Faceplate vertical band proportions
findings.evidence: Photo (x=170 profile): face top seam y218, port window y238-282, bottom edge y291 → top strip 20px = 12mm, window 44px = 26mm, bottom strip 9px = 6mm (CAD front view: ~11.5 / 25 / 8mm). Port band sits BELOW face center.
findings.fix_hint: Place port window band 11.5mm below face top and 6-8mm above face bottom (window height 25-26mm); do not center it vertically.

findings.feature: Per-port LED light-pipe pair
findings.evidence: Photo macro: two rectangular LED windows per port set into the tan bezel at the aperture's outer corners, each ~4px wide x 3px tall = 2.2 x 1.8mm, inset ~1mm from the aperture sides, flanking the latch notch; left lights green, right amber. Bottom row mirrored to bottom corners.
findings.fix_hint: Two 2.2 x 1.8mm emissive windows per port at outer corners (top corners on top row, bottom corners on bottom row); 48+1 ports get pairs, console jack gets none.

findings.feature: SFP latch mid-band hardware
findings.evidence: Photo + CAD: between SFP rows a tan band ~8px = 4.7mm tall spanning cage width with 4 alternating triangular cutouts per cage (down-up-down-up); each TOP cage additionally shows a white horizontal bale-pull rectangle (~10px = 5.8mm wide) at its bottom edge. QSFP band differs: triangles at ends with a row of 4-5 small round holes between; bottom QSFP cage has a wide white pull-tab.
findings.fix_hint: Add a 4.7mm latch band between cage rows: 4 alternating ~2mm triangles per SFP cage + 5.8 x 1.2mm white pull tab per top cage; QSFP band = 2 triangles + 4 O1.5mm holes + 14 x 2mm pull tab on bottom cage.

findings.feature: Reset hole + status LED cluster (top right)
findings.evidence: Photo: dark round hole ~10px = 5-6mm dia (CAD draws ~4.5mm with inner button circle) centered x759 = 434mm from left edge (9mm from right face edge), center ~4-6mm below face top seam; two small LED windows ~2mm to its left; 4 silkscreen labels with leader lines + a rounded-rect outline around the LED pair.
findings.fix_hint: Drill O5mm recessed-button hole at (434mm, 5mm below seam); add 2 O2mm LED dots left of it; silkscreen shapes only (trademark-gated).

findings.feature: Right-side fan vent pocket
findings.evidence: CAD side + CGI: recessed rounded-rect pocket ~35 x 24mm on the RIGHT side panel, vertically centered, spanning ~30-65mm behind the front face (16-22% of 297mm depth); staggered honeycomb round-hole perf (~O3mm, 7 rows); flanked by countersunk screws (vertical pair at pocket rear edge, plus singles at mid-side and rear).
findings.fix_hint: Recess a 35 x 24 x 1mm pocket 30mm behind the face on the right side; fill with staggered O3mm holes at ~4.5mm pitch; add 4-5 countersunk screw heads along the side.

findings.feature: Top lid rib field + embossed text
findings.evidence: CAD top view: 6 stadium-end ribs ~409mm long (0.92 W) x 12mm wide, centers at 26/67/110/182/226/267mm from front edge (3 front, 3 rear, ~42mm spacing, symmetric); outline-font embossed text ~215 x 18mm centered mid-lid between ribs 3 and 4. Visible edge-on in the front photo as faint top-surface relief.
findings.fix_hint: Stamp 6 stadium grooves (r=6mm ends, ~0.5mm deep) at the listed offsets; embossed outline text block 215 x 18mm centered (shapes only).

findings.feature: Rear top-edge hook latches
findings.evidence: CAD top/front views + CGI: two hook/loop tabs on the rear top edge protruding 7mm (the PDF's '7' dim), centered at ~20% and ~73% of body width, each assembly ~28mm wide with a ~15mm loop; 2 screws on the lid 8mm from the rear edge at 147/296mm.
findings.fix_hint: Add two 15mm U-loop tabs rising/curling 7mm past the rear top edge at x=89mm and x=324mm (only matters for 3/4 and top views).

findings.feature: Left label zone silkscreen blocks (trademark-gated, shapes only)
findings.evidence: Photo: wordmark block x33-118px = 13.6-63mm from left edge (49mm long, cap height ~5mm) in the top strip, baseline just above the port window; model-number text block 67-121mm, glyph height ~2.4mm, same line. Nothing below (ports start directly beneath).
findings.fix_hint: Reserve two flat decal rectangles in the top strip: 49 x 5mm at 13.6mm and 54 x 2.4mm at 67mm; no geometry relief.

findings.feature: Port-number silkscreen rows + field rule
findings.evidence: Photo: thin rule line 7.5-8mm below face top seam spanning the RJ45 field, broken at field center (x=182.5mm) by a ~19mm text block; even numbers 2-48 centered above each top port (glyph ~2.5mm, y 233-237), odd 1-47 below bottom ports (~2mm below window). SFP numbers 2/4 above, 1/3 below; QSFP 2 above, 1 below.
findings.fix_hint: Silkscreen plane only: full-field rule with centered text break; 24 numeral blocks above + 24 below at 14mm pitch; per-zone numerals for cages.

findings.feature: Connector-zone bracket outlines (SFP+/40G/console)
findings.evidence: Photo: each right-zone cutout has a rounded-corner open-bottom bracket silkscreen above it with a centered text break ('SFP+' ~x655-668, '40G QSFP+' wider, console label); bracket drops ~3mm at each end; corner r~1mm.
findings.fix_hint: Three bracket outlines: 33mm (SFP), 21mm (QSFP), 14mm (console) wide, in the top strip at the same height as the field rule.

findings.feature: Bottom face / feet line
findings.evidence: CAD side view bottom line is straight — no feet drawn; STH photo shows chassis sitting flush on the table; bottom strip of face is plain except the odd-number row. Tiny tick marks under the CAD front view suggest optional stick-on pads only.
findings.fix_hint: Model bottom flush (no feet); optional 4 low pads <2mm only if a desktop pose is needed.

findings.feature: RJ45 shutter/pin visibility
findings.evidence: Photo macro: cavities are open (no shutters, no dust covers); interior nearly black with faint gold contact glints on the side opposite the latch; visible only at macro distance. Green glow in bottom-row cavities is LED bleed, not geometry.
findings.fix_hint: Empty dark cavity with an 8-contact strip on the anti-latch wall, ~3mm inside; skip below macro LOD.

grades.why: Strong set: near-orthographic 800px front photo (~0.95px/mm on the face), factory CAD 3-view with true dims (443/44.3/297/7), and a 3/4 CGI for sides/top. Gaps: photo blur limits sub-2mm certainty (reset hole 4.5 vs 6mm, LED window shape), ear hole type read from low-res CGI only, no rear view, rib/groove depth unknown, silkscreen text metrics approximate.

Queue (chassis+ears, one-cavity-instanced port grid, cages, LED dots) hits the mass hierarchy but is under-specified: a uniformly instanced single cavity misses the defining 4x12 gang grouping with 5.9mm dividers AND the mirrored bottom row (latch/LED flip); 'cages' must be two distinct types (14.2mm SFP vs 19.4mm QSFP); console+MGMT stack, reset/LED cluster, side vent, and rear hooks are absent from the queue entirely.

Ladder as queued stops at LED dots. Missing rungs: L2.5 gang windows/mullions and band proportions (11.5/25/7mm), L4 cage latch mid-bands with bale pulls and triangle cutouts, right-end cluster, silkscreen rule/bracket/number planes, and the non-front features (vent pocket, lid ribs, rear hooks) that any rack 3/4 shot will expose.

notes: No render exists — this is a catalog pass; every finding is status=missing with severity = how much the feature defines the read. Calibration: front photo face spans x9.5-775px = 443mm (1.728px/mm horizontal, 1.648 vertical from the 73px face height; ~4% anisotropy from slight downward camera tilt, so vertical dims lean on the CAD). Measured aperture columns confirm exactly 24 columns x 2 rows in 4 groups of 6. Ambiguities to resolve before final grading: (1) whether the faceplate has one long window with tan dividers or 4 discrete windows — photo luminance (divider ~100-130 vs face ~220) favors tan gang side-walls with a dark center seam, CAD draws per-gang frames; modeled either way the visible read is tan divider, not white metal. (2) Reset hole diameter: photo ~5.8mm vs CAD ~4.5mm — split at 5mm. (3) Ear holes read as plain rounds, not rack slots — verify before committing (severity 4 item). (4) The 7mm PDF dim belongs to the REAR hooks, not the ear offset; ear flange offset vs face plane is unverified (assumed ~flush). (5) The 'GIGABIT ETHERNET'-position text and all wordmarks are trademark-gated — only their bounding blocks are specced. Angles we lack: true rear, left side, bottom, and any macro close-up sharp enough to confirm latch-notch orientation beyond the CAD+photo agreement (top row notch-up)."

findings.feature: Overall face massing: 2U bezel 432x89mm, grille-left / control-panel-right split
findings.evidence: Both refs agree: louvered grille occupies bezel-span fraction ~0.02-0.74 (CGI x85-1005 of span 65-1372; photo x43-300 of span 32-385), control panel plate occupies 0.78-0.95 (CGI 1082-1288 -> 0.778-0.936; photo 310-368 -> 0.787-0.952 — agreement within 0.02). Face is one full-width molded bezel, no vertical split lines except the panel-flanking pillars.
findings.fix_hint: Build bezel 432 x 89mm; grille window from ~10mm to ~318mm from left edge; panel recess centered at ~374mm from left (0.865 W); flat pillar band ~318-337mm between grille end and panel.

findings.feature: Louvered vent grille: count, pitch, recess
findings.evidence: CGI brightness profile at x580-640 shows 9 bright ridge lines (y~124,148,172,196,220,244,268,293,317) = 8 louver blades, pitch 24.3px = 8.6-8.9mm at 2.75px/mm; grille window y118-330 = 212px = ~75mm of the 89mm face. Photo consistent on pitch (~7mm within blur) but cannot resolve count.
findings.fix_hint: 8 horizontal louvers, pitch 8.7mm, exposed blade face ~3.5mm with ~5mm shadow gap, blades angled down-forward, recessed 3-5mm behind bezel front plane; grille window corner radius 4-6mm (photo shows rounded frame corners).

findings.feature: Control panel plate (recessed rounded square)
findings.evidence: CGI: plate x1082-1288, y130-332 = 208x202px = 76x74mm at 2.73px/mm. Photo: 58x53px at 0.74px/mm = 78x72mm. Two independent refs converge on ~76x74mm, corner radius ~10px CGI = ~4mm, recessed ~1.5-2mm below raised surround.
findings.fix_hint: Plate 76 x 74mm, corner r=4mm, recessed 2mm, left edge at 337mm from bezel left, vertically centered on the face.

findings.feature: Concave scallop pillars flanking the panel recess
findings.evidence: Both refs show a dark vertical trim with a concave highlight arc on EACH side of the panel: CGI left pillar x1040-1078, right crescent x1300-1362 (=14-23mm wide); photo shows the same crescents at x300-310 and x370-380. The right one doubles as the bezel end-cap.
findings.fix_hint: Two vertical concave (C-section) trims, ~15-20mm wide, full plate height, concavity facing the panel; right trim wraps to the bezel right end-cap.

findings.feature: Power button bar at panel top
findings.evidence: CGI: bright-rimmed pill x1092-1272, y152-168 = 180x16px = 66x6mm, sitting in a recessed slot y148-172 (~9mm tall) spanning 0.86 of plate width; power glyph centered at x1182 (~4mm dia). Photo confirms slot position at plate-fraction y 0.08-0.21 but rim far dimmer (CGI idealizes rim contrast — geometry same).
findings.fix_hint: Slot 68x9mm at plate-y 0.08-0.21; raised pill button 66x6mm, full-radius ends, 0.8mm rim chamfer, centered glyph.

findings.feature: Status LED + icon row (4 columns)
findings.evidence: CGI: 4 LED windows 14x5px (5x1.8mm) at centers x1116/1165/1208/1252 — pitch 45px = 0.216 plate width; icon glyphs 20px (~7mm) tall below each at y178-200 (plate-fraction 0.24-0.35). Icons L-to-R: sine-wave online, battery, warning triangle, crossed battery. Photo shows same 4-column layout with leftmost lit green.
findings.fix_hint: 4 columns at plate-x fractions ~0.17/0.40/0.61/0.83; each = 5x1.8mm LED slot above a 7mm printed icon; only geometry is the LED slot recess — icons are flat graphics.

findings.feature: LCD window boundary and emissive plane
findings.evidence: CGI hides the glass boundary entirely (window blends into plate); the photo shows a faintly distinct darker rectangle with glow bleed around the blue text, spanning nearly full plate width, ~14px/66 = ~19mm tall. Text 'Smart-UPS 1500' at CGI y218-242 = plate-fraction 0.43-0.55, char height ~6mm dot-matrix. This CGI-vs-photo disagreement is evidence the CGI idealizes the window flush.
findings.fix_hint: Model a flush dark glass window ~62 x 20mm centered on plate x, top edge at plate-y ~0.40; emissive blue 5x7 dot-matrix layer inset 0.5mm behind glass, with bloom permitted at material stage.

findings.feature: Button cluster 2x2 (Esc / Enter / Up / Down)
findings.evidence: CGI: left column x1092-1175, right column x1190-1272 (each 82px = 30mm wide), rows y272-296 and y300-324 (24px = 8.8mm tall), column gap 15px = 5.5mm, row gap 4px = 1.5mm, at plate-fraction y 0.70-0.96. Photo confirms 4 buttons, same positions, dimmer rims. Esc top-left, return-arrow bottom-left, up chevron top-right, down chevron bottom-right.
findings.fix_hint: 4 raised buttons 30 x 8.8mm, corner r=2mm, raised ~1.2mm with 0.8mm rim chamfer; grid: 5.5mm column gap, 1.5mm row gap, cluster bottom edge 3mm above plate bottom.

findings.feature: Rack ears: profile and mounting-hole pattern
findings.evidence: Both refs show flat black L-bracket ears full 2U height with 4 obround holes in two pairs (top pair + bottom pair; CGI left ear holes at y~75/125/255/305 of face 60-345; photo confirms 4-hole pattern). NOT in the planned queue (chassis/LCD/buttons/vents/badge). Flange width DISAGREES: CGI reads ~10mm (28px at ~2.75px/mm), photo reads ~18mm (18px at ~1px/mm); EIA standard is ~15.9mm — CGI likely thins it.
findings.fix_hint: Add ears to the build queue: flange 16 x 88mm, 4 obround holes ~9.5 x 7mm, 15.9mm spacing within each pair, pairs symmetric about mid-height; 2-3mm corner chamfers; rearward return with hem.

findings.feature: Grille frame bands (top/bottom margin thickness)
findings.evidence: Direct conflict: CGI top band 18px/250 = 7% of face height, bottom ~6%; photo top band ~14px/82 = 14-16%, bottom ~15%. Photo blur may merge the shadowed outermost louver into the band, and the CGI may idealize the grille extent — cannot resolve at 440px.
findings.fix_hint: Provisionally 9mm top band / 9mm bottom band (10% H each, grille ~71mm); flag for a straight-on frontal reference before locking; do not copy the CGI's 6-7mm bands without a tiebreaker.

findings.feature: Left stepped strip (louver end-returns at grille left termination)
findings.evidence: CGI ear crop shows a bright vertical zigzag of ~9-11 steps at x70-95 where the louvers die into the bezel left frame; the photo shows the same bright stepped strip at x36-42, y158-202. Both refs agree it reads bright (edge-lit steps), ~3-4mm wide.
findings.fix_hint: Terminate each louver blade with a stepped return into the left frame: one step per louver (8 steps + boundary edges), step depth ~3mm, strip width 3-4mm, located 8-12mm from bezel left edge.

findings.feature: Vertical support-web columns behind louvers (tick marks)
findings.evidence: CGI shows small bright vertical dashes in every louver gap at ~5 evenly spaced columns (x~205/390/570/750/930, spacing ~183px = ~65mm), dividing the grille into 6 bays. Photo cannot resolve them (single-source evidence, but such webs are structurally necessary in a molded grille).
findings.fix_hint: 5 vertical web columns at 65mm spacing, web 1.5-2mm thick, set 2-3mm behind the louver faces so they read as dashes in each gap.

findings.feature: Badge zone (APC mark) — trademark-gated bounding volume
findings.evidence: Chrome beveled letters bridging louvers. Box: CGI x150-275, y185-235 -> ~45mm wide x 18mm letter height, center at 0.45 H; photo x75-123, y165-180, center at ~0.36-0.39 H. Horizontal-position fractions disagree (CGI center 0.11 W vs photo 0.19 W) — perspective residual; true left edge ~30-40mm from bezel left.
findings.fix_hint: Reserve a 45 x 19mm raised badge volume, left edge 35mm from bezel left, center at 0.40 H (slightly above mid-face), proud of louvers by ~1.5mm; keep as neutral geometry — no trademark glyphs until gate clears.

findings.feature: Front-panel split lines / bezel-to-chassis seams
findings.evidence: Both refs: single-piece bezel with perimeter seam to the metal chassis; top cover overhangs with a thin bright lip line (photo y~145); left flank shows a stepped return where bezel meets chassis (CGI x62-85). No mid-face vertical splits.
findings.fix_hint: One bezel part; 1-2mm reveal seam at perimeter; top cover front edge overhangs bezel ~2mm with visible hem line; left/right bezel flanks return rearward with ~10-15deg draft and large corner wrap radius.

findings.feature: Top-cover circular openings (visible in 3/4 views)
findings.evidence: CGI renders 6 pure-black circular voids (~60-70px = 22-25mm dia); the photo shows ~5 shallow GRAY circles with visible interior floors — the disagreement exposes CGI idealization (voids vs shallow recessed openings). Positions roughly match between refs.
findings.fix_hint: Model as recessed circular openings ~24mm dia with interior floor geometry (not black decals, not through-voids); confirm count (5 vs 6) from a top-down reference before final.

findings.feature: Breaker access on the front face
findings.evidence: Absent in both references — no breaker button, no circular breaker relief anywhere on the front bezel (rear-panel feature on this model). Confirmed in CGI panel crop and photo front crop.
findings.fix_hint: Do NOT add a front breaker; keep the face limited to grille + panel cluster.

findings.feature: Foot line
findings.evidence: Rackmount unit: no feet in either reference; bottom front edge is a flat band with a thin dark chassis lip below the bezel (CGI y345-352).
findings.fix_hint: No feet; flat bottom band with a 2-3mm chassis lip visible below the bezel at the front.

grades.why: Two references but both are 3/4-left views from the same quadrant; one is vendor CGI (layout-only trust) and the real photo is 440px — too soft to resolve louver count, web columns, or recess depths. Three measured conflicts remain open (frame-band thickness 7% vs 15% H, ear flange 10 vs 18mm, badge x-fraction 0.11 vs 0.19). No straight-on front, no panel macro, no top-down. The photography gap is now documented feature-by-feature.

The queue (chassis, LCD emissive, buttons, vents, badge) covers the five dominant read-defining zones, but the catalog surfaced uncovered features: rack ears (sev 4, define the rackmount read), scallop pillars flanking the panel, power bar + LED/icon row (only implicitly under 'buttons'/'LCD'), the stepped louver-end strip, and split lines. Ears are the largest omission.

Tiers 0-3 (massing, grille window, panel plate, controls) are specifiable from this catalog with mm values. Tier 4 micro-geometry is only half-anchored: web columns and LCD window boundary are single-source (CGI-only / photo-only respectively), and the two depth-critical numbers (grille recess depth, plate recess depth) are estimated, not measured — no raking-angle reference exists to pin them.

notes: No render exists yet — every 'missing' status means 'reference feature awaiting modeling', with fix_hint doubling as the build spec. Measurement method: local face height = 89mm (2U) as scale anchor (CGI 2.73-2.81 px/mm, photo 0.74-0.92 px/mm); horizontal positions given as fractions of bezel span to cancel perspective — validated by CGI/photo agreement within 0.02 on the panel plate. CGI-vs-photo disagreements found (each marks a CGI idealization or a photo-resolution floor): (1) frame-band thickness 7% vs 15% of H — unresolved, needs frontal shot; (2) ear flange width ~10mm CGI vs ~18mm photo — spec 16mm EIA, CGI thins it; (3) LCD glass boundary invisible in CGI, faintly present with glow bleed in photo — trust photo; (4) chrome rims on power bar/buttons far brighter in CGI — material idealization, geometry unaffected; (5) top-cover circles are black voids in CGI, shallow gray recesses in photo — trust photo; (6) badge x-position fractions disagree by 0.08 span. Reference acquisition wishlist, priority order: straight-on frontal (locks band ratios + badge x), macro of the display cluster (locks recess depths + LCD window edge), top-down (locks circle count), right-end 3/4 (end-cap wrap, currently occluded by rails in CGI). Trademark gate: APC letters and the four status icons cataloged as bounding volumes/flat graphics only. Working crops saved in the session scratchpad (cgi_panel_grid.png, cgi_slat_strip.png, photo_grid.png, etc.) for the modeling session to reuse.

findings.feature: 1U metal blanking panel — overall format (flat face, screw-mount)
findings.evidence: blankb1_front.jpg: single flat steel panel, no face relief/emboss, one oval mounting slot per end vertically centered, long edges folded back into stiffening returns. Macro confirms powder-coated steel with folded hem. CAUTION: apparent length:height in the front photo measures ~6-7:1 after perspective, closer to a 2U (5.4:1 true) than a 1U (10.9:1 true); the macro slot-to-face ratio (~65px slot width vs >600px face span) also leans 2U. Filename says b1.
findings.fix_hint: Model 482.6 x 44.45 x ~1.5mm steel sheet, face dead flat. If the SKU turns out 2U, keep slot (10 x 6.4mm) and return depth ABSOLUTE and scale only face height to 44.45mm for the 1U build. Corner radii ~2mm.

findings.feature: Cable duct — finger count, pitch, slot profile
findings.evidence: cableduct1u_front.jpg: front face carries ~20 evenly pitched vertical slots (count ±2 at this resolution) between fingers; slot width ≈ 0.40-0.45 of pitch; slots run full face height with small (~2-3mm) corner radii, closed at top by the cover plane. This periodic slot rhythm is the duct's entire read at mid distance.
findings.fix_hint: 21 fingers / 20 slots across the 482.6mm body: pitch ≈ 23mm, slot ≈ 9-10mm wide, finger ≈ 13mm. Slot corner radius 2.5mm. Duct body projects ~60-75mm forward of the flange, body height ≈ 40mm (fits inside 1U). Verify exact count against ref before freezing — an off-by-two pitch error is visible.

findings.feature: Blanking panel — edge returns and end relief notches
findings.evidence: blankb1_front.jpg bottom edge shows a folded-back return lip (~25px vs ~200px face, so ~10-12% of face height) with a visible relief notch at the left end where the flange stops short of the panel end; blankb1_macro_cagenuts.jpg shows the fold as a doubled hem line at the edge. Without the return the panel reads as a paper-thin plane.
findings.fix_hint: Fold both long edges back 90 deg, return depth 9-10mm, inner bend radius ~1.5mm (1x sheet). Terminate the return 12-15mm before each panel end with a square relief notch so the ends sit flat on the rails.

findings.feature: Blanking panel — mounting slots (count, shape, position)
findings.evidence: One oval slot per end, vertically centered, long axis horizontal; macro measures slot ≈ 110 x 65px = 1.7:1 aspect (standard 10 x 6.4mm = 1.56:1, consistent within perspective error). Slot center inset ~2-3% of panel length from each end.
findings.fix_hint: Two slots total: 10mm x 6.4mm obround, full-radius ends, centered on face height, centers 465mm apart (inset ~9mm from each end). NOT round holes — the obround is what a rack-experienced eye checks.

findings.feature: Cage nut — face geometry (square cage + extruded collar + thread)
findings.evidence: cagenut_single_macro.jpg face-on: square body ~215px wide; raised circular collar OD ≈ 160px = 0.74 of body width; countersunk funnel down to thread hole ≈ 90px = 0.42 of body width (M6 ≈ 6mm/12.5mm face = 0.48, matches within measurement error). Square corner radii ≈ 0.07 of width. Sides fold back wrapping the loose square nut, visible as a step in the in-context macro.
findings.fix_hint: Face plate 12.5mm square, corner R 0.9mm; extruded collar OD 9.5mm raised ~0.8mm with a conical countersink into the M6 (6mm nominal) thread bore; loose square nut 10 x 10 x 4mm captive behind, visibly separate from the cage through the side gaps.

findings.feature: Cage nut — spring wings with castellated (split) tips
findings.evidence: Both macros: two opposing spring wings fold back from the face plate over the nut; each wing tip is split into two prongs by a center notch ≈ 0.26 of body width, and the tips protrude ~5% beyond the square silhouette top and bottom. In blankb1_macro_cagenuts.jpg the wing arc over the nut body is clearly visible in 3/4 view — this arc plus split tip is THE identifying signature of a cage nut.
findings.fix_hint: Two wings, one per opposite side, folding back at ~60-70 deg then arcing over the nut; wing tip split by a 3.2mm center notch into two 2.5mm prongs; tips reach ~1mm past the face-plate silhouette. Zinc bright finish micro-grain.

findings.feature: Cage nut placement rule + bare square holes
findings.evidence: References establish the hardware system (9.5mm square rail holes implied by the cage geometry) but the rule must be encoded: nuts appear ONLY at occupied mounting positions; every other rail hole is an empty 9.5mm square with visible punched edge. A rack with nuts in every hole, or none, fails instantly to an experienced viewer.
findings.fix_hint: 1U blank: 1 nut per rail at the CENTER hole of the U. Duct (2 slots/ear): nuts at TOP and BOTTOM holes of the U (+/-15.88mm from U center). All unoccupied holes: bare 9.5mm squares, ~0.3mm edge break. Never populate empty-U holes.

findings.feature: M6 mounting screws (two head styles, flanged washer, thread)
findings.evidence: blankb1_macro_cagenuts.jpg shows TWO screw geometries shipped with the panel: a screw with integrated flat washer/flange (upper right, standing on its washer) and a domed truss-head Phillips (lower center); both zinc, visible ~1mm thread pitch, length ≈ 2x head diameter (M6 x 12). The build queue lists cage nuts but NO screws — yet every occupied nut position on a mounted panel is visually a screw head, not a nut face.
findings.fix_hint: Add M6 x 12 truss-head Phillips: head OD 12-13mm, dome height ~2.5mm, Phillips #3 recess; optional 14mm integrated washer variant. At every occupied hole render screw head OVER the slot; bare cage-nut faces are only visible at pre-staged empty positions.

findings.feature: PDU — full-length 0U geometry (outlets, breakers, mounting)
findings.evidence: ap8641_pdu.png is a tight crop of the controller pod only: no outlets, no bank breakers, no length, no mounting pegs. At orbit distance a 0U PDU reads via (a) the continuous ~56mm-wide black extrusion strip, (b) the periodic outlet-cluster pitch, (c) bank separators, (d) the display glow — the reference covers only (d). Cannot be modeled truthfully from this ref.
findings.fix_hint: Either acquire a full-length AP8641 reference (front + 3/4) before modeling, or restrict the PDU to non-hero placement (rear corner, facing away) where only the extrusion silhouette and pod glow are visible. Do not invent outlet spacing.

findings.feature: Toolless plastic blanking panel (second variant in refs)
findings.evidence: blankb1_q34.jpg is NOT the same product as the metal panel: molded plastic bar, deep top return (~20mm), no screw slots, small snap-clip tabs at the ends (stepped tab lower-right, notched tab upper-left) — a toolless snap-in blank. The reference set conflates two products under one name; this variant is what actually covers the 'toolless clip tabs' item in the focus.
findings.fix_hint: Model as a separate asset: 1U molded blank, flat front face, top flange returning ~20mm, end snap tabs ~8 x 5mm engaging the 9.5mm square holes directly (no cage nuts, no screws). Decide per rack position: metal+screws OR toolless — never mix geometry from both on one panel.

findings.feature: Cable duct — removable top cover
findings.evidence: cableduct1u_front.jpg shows the cover installed: a flat plane closing the duct top, full length, flush with the finger tops, front edge forming a crisp second horizontal line above the slot field. Cover-on vs cover-off changes the whole silhouette.
findings.fix_hint: Flat cover, full 482.6mm length, ~1.5-2mm visible edge thickness, sitting flush on the finger tops with a ~1mm front reveal line. Model as a separate detachable body so a cables-visible variant is possible later.

findings.feature: Cable duct — rack ears with two slots each
findings.evidence: Bottom-left ear in the duct ref shows the flat mounting flange with two small obround slots stacked vertically; top-right ear matches. Two-point mounting per side is what puts cage nuts at the top+bottom holes of the U.
findings.fix_hint: Flange 44.45mm tall per side, two 10 x 6.4mm obround slots per ear at +/-15.88mm from U centerline, slot centers 465mm apart across the unit.

findings.feature: Powder-coat orange-peel micro texture (blank + duct)
findings.evidence: blankb1_macro_cagenuts.jpg resolves a fine orange-peel bump across the black face — at macro distance a geometrically smooth face reads as plastic/CG even with correct shading. This is geometry (surface relief), not material tone.
findings.fix_hint: Procedural bump: cell/noise scale ~0.4-0.7mm feature size, amplitude ~0.02-0.04mm, on all powder-coated steel faces (metal blank, duct body). The plastic toolless blank gets a finer, tighter mold texture instead.

findings.feature: Blanking panel 'relief' face variant (in build queue)
findings.evidence: The queue plans 'blanking flat+relief' but NO reference shows an embossed/relieved blank — both blank refs are flat-faced. Modeling a relief profile now would be invention, exactly what this pipeline forbids.
findings.fix_hint: Acquire a reference for the relieved blank (e.g. the common trapezoidal center emboss type) before modeling it, or drop 'relief' from the queue and ship flat-only. Do not extrapolate a relief profile.

findings.feature: PDU controller pod (LCD, buttons, port cluster) — the one modelable PDU region
findings.evidence: ap8641_pdu.png: recessed LCD ≈ 0.64 of face width (face ~165px, LCD ~105px wide), ~1.1:1 aspect, with MAIN/?/SELECT soft-label row at its base; three domed round buttons below, dia ≈ 0.13 of face width, evenly spaced; below that a small LED pair and a recessed port cluster of 4-5 jacks (two RJ45 left column, two sensor RJ right column, USB at bottom right), each in its own rectangular cutout; extrusion edges chamfered with a side channel groove.
findings.fix_hint: On a 56mm-wide extrusion face: LCD window 36 x 33mm recessed 1.5mm; three 7mm domed buttons on 12mm pitch; port cutouts ~16 x 13mm (RJ45) in a 2-column block; 2mm edge chamfers and a longitudinal T-slot groove on each side face.

findings.feature: Thumbscrews
findings.evidence: Focus lists thumbscrews but no reference image contains one — the hardware macro shows only truss/washer-head machine screws. No knurl profile, head diameter, or captive-collar geometry can be sourced from this set.
findings.fix_hint: Either add a thumbscrew reference (knurled M6, typical head OD 9.5-11mm, knurl pitch ~1mm) or remove thumbscrews from this face's spec; the referenced accessories are all plain-screw or toolless.

findings.feature: Cage nut rear/side depth (wing spring curvature behind the rail)
findings.evidence: Both cage-nut refs are face-on or shallow 3/4; the behind-rail wing extension and nut standoff depth (~6-7mm total behind the face) are only inferable, not measurable. Matters only if the camera ever sees rail rear.
findings.fix_hint: If rail rears enter frame, source one side-profile cage-nut reference; otherwise model total depth 7mm behind face plate and lock rear detail at low priority.

grades.why: Cage nut coverage is excellent (clean face-on macro + in-context 3/4 with shipping hardware) and the metal blank has three usable views including a texture-resolving macro. But: the two 'blankb1' refs conflate a metal screw-mount and a plastic toolless product; the front photo's proportions measure closer to 2U than 1U (needs SKU confirmation); the duct has a single 3/4 view (rear pass-through and cover underside unknown); the PDU ref is a pod-only crop useless for full-length geometry; and thumbscrews plus the planned 'relief' blank have zero reference.

Queue (blanking flat+relief, duct fingers+cover, cage nuts at occupied holes) hits the three mid-tier assets, but is misaligned with the reference set in both directions: it plans a relief blank that has no reference (invention risk), while omitting the M6 screws that visually replace nut faces at every occupied hole, the toolless plastic blank that IS referenced, and any decision on the PDU. 'Cage nuts at occupied holes' is the right rule but incomplete without the screw-head-over-slot rule.

Macro rung is strong (cage nut geometry, split wing tips, screw heads, orange-peel texture all sourced). Mid rung is adequate (blank profile, duct rhythm, ear slots). Orbit rung is broken: the 0U PDU — the largest accessory silhouette in any rack orbit — cannot be built from the pod crop, and no reference establishes the empty-square-hole rail field that frames all these parts at distance. Ladder currently ends one rung short of the orbit read.

notes: Render side is empty by design (unmodeled face) — all 'missing' findings are the build spec, ordered by how strongly each feature sells rack truth. Key reference caveats: (1) blankb1_front.jpg proportions suggest the photographed panel may be 2U (apparent L:H ~6-7:1 vs 10.9:1 for a true 1U even allowing perspective; macro slot-to-face ratio agrees) — confirm the SKU and, if 2U, keep slot/return/hem dimensions absolute and scale only face height; (2) blankb1_q34.jpg is a different product (plastic toolless snap blank) than the metal panel in the other two blank refs — treat as two assets, never merge their features; (3) PDU orbit-relevance answer: at orbit distance a 0U PDU reads through outlet pitch, bank separators, and the continuous extrusion strip — none of which the AP8641 crop provides; either source a full-length reference or place the PDU facing away/non-hero; (4) no thumbscrew and no relieved-blank reference exists — both must be sourced or cut from the queue before modeling. Missing angles that would close remaining gaps: cage nut side profile (wing depth), duct rear/underside, any full-length PDU view.

findings.feature: Front caster pair (twin-wheel swivel casters) below base band
findings.evidence: g12 front elevation (cabinet outer width W=496px = 750mm, 1.51mm/px): two caster assemblies visible below the bottom band, left assembly spans x537-600 (63px = 0.127W ~ 95mm), right mirrored at x902-965. Each shows TWO dark wheels with a light-gray yoke between (wheel gap ~8px ~ 12mm). Wheels project ~27px (~41mm) below the band bottom edge (band bottom y~1390, wheel bottom y~1417). Not in the stated frame plan (posts, rails, top, base, door only). FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES - they define the grounded read.
findings.fix_hint: Add 4 twin-wheel swivel casters: wheel dia ~65mm x ~20mm wide each, 12mm gap between the pair, yoke center 95mm inboard of each side face, wheel bottoms 40mm below the base-band bottom edge (they are the floor contact).

findings.feature: Base band (plinth) proportion + full-width floor shadow line
findings.evidence: g12: bottom band is 35px tall (~53mm, 0.026 of H=1328px) spanning full W, bottom edge ~41mm above floor with wheels bridging the gap; a continuous dark floor-shadow band runs under the full width (rows ~1398-1420) even between casters. If the current 'base' is a box sitting on the floor plane, the read is wrong: the cabinet floats on casters. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES.
findings.fix_hint: Base band 53mm tall, full 750mm width, bottom face 40mm above floor plane; ground contact only at the 4 casters (plus retracted feet). Keep an unobstructed 40mm air gap under the band so the AO shadow line forms.

findings.feature: Leveling feet at the four base corners (retracted)
findings.evidence: g12: light-gray pads at the extreme corners, x505-522 and x985-1000 (pad ~17px ~ 26mm visible front-on), pad bottoms ~y1400, i.e. ~26mm ABOVE wheel contact (feet retracted, wheels carrying). g16 macro confirms a round mushroom-profile black foot with visible thread stem under the corner block. Both feet and casters coexist on the real unit. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES (corner silhouette).
findings.fix_hint: 4 leveling feet at corner extremes (center ~20mm inboard of each side/front face): pad dia 45mm, pad height 12mm, M12-scale stem; model retracted with pad bottom 25mm above floor so casters remain the contact.

findings.feature: Door hinge blocks and latch keepers left on post faces (door-off presentation)
findings.evidence: g12 left post outer face: raised hinge blocks at y~0.16H and ~0.77H, each ~56px tall (~85mm) x ~20px (~30mm) with 2 screws; obround latch-keeper plates ~34px (~50mm) at ~0.24H and ~0.71H with twin round bosses; small bright clip at mid-height ~0.46H on BOTH posts. The plan's 'door built-but-open' implies hinges exist somewhere, but the post-face hardware is absent from the frame spec. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES (proud of the post face, catches light).
findings.fix_hint: On left post face add 2 hinge blocks 85x30x12mm proud, centered at 16% and 77% of height, 2 dome screws each; add obround keepers 50x20x6mm at 24% and 71%; add one 20x12mm clip at 46% height on each post.

findings.feature: Corner gusset 'castle' plates with square perforation pattern at all 4 post ends
findings.evidence: g12: at each corner of the front opening a gray plate ~35px (~53mm) tall sits just behind the post, carrying a distinctive pattern of ~6 small square perforations (~4px ~ 6mm each) in an offset 2x3 grid; visible top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right. g13 confirms the plate obliquely with its fixing screws. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES (all four corners of the opening).
findings.fix_hint: Add a 53mm-tall x ~45mm plate flush 5mm behind each post face at top and bottom of the opening, with six 6mm square through-holes in an offset 2-row grid.

findings.feature: Dark brush/air-dam strip inboard of each post (vertical, full height)
findings.evidence: g12: a near-black vertical band x~532-560 (28px ~ 42mm) runs the FULL interior height directly behind the left post face, with a fuzzy (filament) edge visible at top; mirrored on the right. It is the strongest dark vertical in the front view after the posts themselves. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES.
findings.fix_hint: Add a 42mm-wide full-height near-black strip in the plane ~10mm behind each post face, with 2-3mm irregular edge displacement to read as brush filaments at close range.

findings.feature: Powder-coat orange-peel texture on all painted faces
findings.evidence: g16 macro: pronounced orange-peel/wrinkle relief on the black coat - bump pitch ~0.5-1mm, clearly breaking specular highlights across the corner block and band; the memory/plan notes the current frame render is smooth. Geometry-adjacent (surface relief), and it is what separates 'CG box' from 'steel cabinet' at any close pass. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES at close range.
findings.fix_hint: Apply procedural bump/displacement: feature pitch 0.6mm, amplitude ~0.05mm, isotropic, on every painted steel face (posts, band, roof fascia, door frame); exclude zinc/silver hardware.

findings.feature: Bottom band face details: slot row + 2 pan screws + 2 top-face cutouts
findings.evidence: g12 base crop: a row of small rectangular slots along the band's bottom edge (slot ~4px tall ~ 6mm, pitch ~22px ~ 33mm, ~15 across the width); two silver pan-head screws on the band face at x~0.32W and ~0.67W at ~65% of band height; two small rectangular cutouts on the band's top horizontal face near center (x~0.45W, 0.54W). FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES (screws glint; slot row shades the bottom edge).
findings.fix_hint: On the 53mm band: slot row 20x6mm at 33mm pitch, 8mm above bottom edge, full width; 8mm-dia screw heads at 240mm and 500mm from left edge, 34mm below band top; two 20x8mm cutouts on the top face at mid-span.

findings.feature: Rail-flank tool-less cable loops / T-slot features (repeating up the rail web)
findings.evidence: g12 left-post crop: a repeating stamped loop/T feature runs up the dark rail flank at pitch ~83px ~ 2.8U (~125mm), ~14 repeats full height; g15 confirms T-shaped punchouts on the rail web at the same rhythm. Plan covers 'rails+holes' (the EIA square holes) but not this second feature family on the flank. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES through the open front, especially at oblique angles.
findings.fix_hint: On each rail's side web add a stamped loop/T cutout ~25x15mm every 3U (133mm), 14 per rail, aligned to U boundaries.

findings.feature: Top front cross-member slot row (under roof, inside opening)
findings.evidence: g12 top crop: small rectangular slots along the lower edge of the inner top cross member, pitch ~27px (~41mm), slot ~12x4mm, running the interior width; also 2 fasteners on the roof front edge at ~0.28W and ~0.72W. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES (in shadow, but reads as a serration at the opening's top edge).
findings.fix_hint: Add 12x4mm slots at 41mm pitch along the top cross member's bottom edge, full interior width; add two 8mm fastener heads on the roof front edge at 28% and 72% of W.

findings.feature: Side panel front seam / reveal + bottom corner counterbored screw (3/4 orbit)
findings.evidence: g15: the side panel plane sits inboard of the post outer face with a thin vertical reveal behind the front frame; g16 macro: side/base corner trim carries a round counterbore (~3x screw-head dia) with a recessed silver Phillips screw, plus a horizontal seam between trim and panel. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: YES at 3/4 angles only; invisible dead-on.
findings.fix_hint: Set side plane 10mm inboard of post outer face with a 4mm reveal gap; at each bottom corner add a 22mm-dia x 4mm counterbore with 9mm screw head, 25mm from each edge; add horizontal trim seam ~55mm above panel bottom.

findings.feature: Roof top-surface features: 4 brush-strip cable entries, perforation rows, NO STEP mark
findings.evidence: g15 roof crop: four glossy-black brush slots near the four roof corners (each ~0.25-0.3 of the adjacent edge length, ~60-70mm wide), several rows of small holes/slots across the panel, red 'NO STEP' roundel at center, roof field slightly recessed between raised edge rails. At an eye-level front orbit of a 1991mm cabinet NONE of this is visible - only the plain 33mm front fascia (g12: rows 92-114 = 22px). FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: NO (fascia only), unless the orbit elevates above ~10 degrees.
findings.fix_hint: Per front-facing doctrine: model only the 33mm plain fascia + its 2 edge fasteners. If any elevated shot enters the sequence, add 4 brush slots (300x65mm, corner-adjacent, inset 40mm from edges) and a 2mm-recessed roof field.

findings.feature: Rail-to-frame depth-adjust brackets (rail top/bottom L-brackets)
findings.evidence: g13: an L-bracket ties the rail top to the top frame with a single large screw and a stamped lock/unlock + arrow icon field; g14 plan view shows the 4 bracket assemblies with slotted depth-adjust arms. From a front orbit these are deep in shadow behind the corner castle plates; only fragments visible at strong obliques. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: MARGINAL (mostly no).
findings.fix_hint: Low priority: a 60x40x3mm L-bracket at each rail end with one 10mm screw boss is sufficient as a silhouette filler behind the corner plates; skip the icon stamping.

findings.feature: Vertical rail U-number markings
findings.evidence: No reference resolves numerals: g12 renders the rail strip as alternating square holes only (1500px full-height frame gives ~0.9mm/px - a 5mm numeral is sub-2px); g13/g15 macros show the rail web and T-slots but crop away the numbered face. Real SX rails carry printed U numbers, but shape/position/font cannot be grounded from these five photos. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: in principle yes at close range.
findings.fix_hint: Either source one close-up of the rail face before modeling, or apply a generic 6mm-tall numeral decal strip every 44.45mm and flag it as ungrounded in the claims ledger.

findings.feature: Side-panel top latch + serrated grounding strip (roof-area hardware)
findings.evidence: g13: toothed silver grounding rack along the side-panel top edge (tooth pitch ~10-12mm), sliding latch block with large slotted cam screw and lock/unlock icons, punched circle/square/diamond shapes in the strip. All of it sits under the roof line, visible only with the camera above the cabinet or the roof off. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: NO.
findings.fix_hint: Skip per front-facing doctrine. If a top-down shot is ever added: toothed strip 12mm pitch x 8mm tooth height along panel top rail, latch block 45x30mm with 20mm cam screw.

findings.feature: Base shipping/bolt-down brackets (g14 plan view)
findings.evidence: g14 (underside/plan reference) shows four large angled brackets bolted at the base corners with load-rating labels - these are shipping/bolt-down fittings, not the standard floor-standing presentation, and are invisible from any front orbit. Risk is modeling them from this ref and polluting the silhouette. FRONT-ORBIT-VISIBLE: NO.
findings.fix_hint: Do NOT model. Treat g14 as evidence for base-frame plan geometry (member widths, caster/foot positions) only.

grades.why: Five views cover the front elevation door-off (g12, orthographic enough to scale at 1.51mm/px against the 750mm width), an underside/plan (g14), an elevated 3/4 with roof and open door (g15), and two macros (g13 corner hardware, g16 base corner + foot + texture). Measured W/H = 496/1328px = 0.373 matches the 750x1991mm AR3140 within 1%, so the set is internally consistent. Gaps: no legible U-numeral close-up, no ground-level macro of caster/foot from the front, no clean side elevation, no rear; g14's view direction (top vs bottom) needs one caption check before its brackets are used dimensionally.

The stated plan (posts, rails+holes, top, base, door built-but-open) covers primary massing only. Of the front-orbit-visible feature families the refs establish, nine are absent from the plan: casters (sev4), floating base band + floor gap (sev4), leveling feet, hinge/keeper hardware, corner castle plates, brush strips, band face details, rail-flank loops, orange-peel texture. The base as planned likely sits on the floor plane, which contradicts the reference's caster-borne stance - a massing-level error, not just detail.

Ladder has three rungs: massing (present), mid-scale hardware 20-100mm (empty - hinges, feet, casters, plates, screws, slots all missing), and macro surface 0.5-2mm (empty - powder-coat orange peel, brush filaments). Only the top rung exists; the render will read as CG at any distance closer than a full-cabinet wide shot. The 33mm roof fascia being correctly plain is the one place the doctrine lets the plan off cheaply.

notes: No render frames were provided - per the brief this is a build-spec catalog, so 'missing' means absent from the stated frame plan, not verified against pixels of a render. Scale basis: g12 outer width 496px = 750mm (AR3140 is the 750mm-wide 42U SX; measured W/H ratio confirms within 1%) giving 1.51mm/px on that view; all mm figures derive from it and carry roughly +/-5% from edge softness. Angles we lack: legible rail U-numerals (finding marked unverifiable - do not invent), ground-level front macro of the caster/foot zone (my 41mm wheel exposure and 26mm foot lift come from a soft shadow boundary), true side elevation, rear. g14 shows corner brackets that are shipping/bolt-down fittings - excluded from the spec deliberately. Priority order for the fix loop if budget is tight: casters + floating base band (the two sev-4 items define the grounded silhouette front-on), then post-face hardware and castle plates (the only glinting mid-scale features in a dead-front view), then orange-peel texture before any close pass. Reference files: /Users/scammermike/Downloads/computexchange/.claude/worktrees/rack-oracle/render/ref/rack/enclosure-photos/ar3140_gallery_12.jpg through _16.jpg.

findings.feature: Fill-map with deliberate void (gap system)
findings.evidence: All 4 unique racks are 60-85% filled with exactly one dominant void in the upper-middle third, never at the bottom. xtremeownage_rack_front: empty band spans ~0.14 of interior height (~4U of ~25U), centered at ~0.45 from top, open rails visible through to the wall. shawnmix: ~2-3U air gap above the twin-tower shelf at ~0.55 height, rack ~65% full. cavelab_2019: mesh-backed partial 4U void between the server triplet and bottom UPS. Current plan has NO fill/gap parameter; a uniformly-populated rack is the loudest single showroom tell.
findings.fix_hint: Add a fill-map generator: total fill 0.60-0.85 of usable U; place exactly one contiguous void of 2-5U with its center in the 0.30-0.55 height band (from top); void shows bare rails (open frame) or dark cavity (cabinet), never a blanking panel.

findings.feature: Bottom-heavy mass gradient
findings.evidence: Heaviest/deepest units form a contiguous bottom block in 4/4 racks: cavelab_2019 UPS at 0.83-0.95 height; cavelab_2024 two 12-bay servers + 2U UPS as bottom three units; shawnmix 1U PDU + 2U LCD UPS as bottom 3U; xtremeownage three 2U 12-bay Dells (R730XD/R720XD + LogRhythm) as bottom ~6U with PowerVault directly above. UPS is the bottom-most powered unit in 3/4.
findings.fix_hint: Constrain placement: deepest chassis class (2U+ hot-swap servers, disk shelves, UPS) fills the bottom 0.20-0.30 of rack height contiguously; UPS is the lowest unit; unit visual mass must monotonically decrease (with noise) from bottom block to top band.

findings.feature: Front-crossing patch-cable arcs (panel-to-switch catenaries)
findings.evidence: The plan's 'restrained cabling' contradicts 4/6 photos. dimitrije: 5 blue arcs from keystone panel down ~1.5U into a switch, matched lengths, apex protruding proud of the faces, cable diameter ~0.016 of opening width, evenly spaced across ~55% of width. shawnmix: fully populated 24-port panel with ~20+ tight orange loops in two crossing rows. xtremeownage: two lone black cables crossing diagonally IN FRONT of a 1U brush panel. Front-face cable crossings are the signature of an owned rack, not a defect.
