findings.fix_hint: Replace 'restrained' with a two-tier rule: (a) 4-24 matched catenary arcs, sag 1-2U, one saturated hue, connecting the top patch panel to the switch 1-2U below, loop apexes 30-60mm proud of the face plane; (b) 1-2 'sin' cables in black crossing a lower panel diagonally. Zero front cables on the bottom server block.

findings.feature: Shelf-mounted non-rackmount consumer gear
findings.evidence: 4/4 racks carry consumer boxes on cantilever shelves: cavelab_2024 has 4 shelves of HP/Lenovo SFF+mini PCs plus a white RIPE probe; dimitrije has 4 Dell OptiPlex micros each spanning only ~0.61 of the opening with mesh filler right; shawnmix shelves a Raspberry Pi, flat white hub, and two rounded black towers; xtremeownage shelves a 4-bay Synology plus two vertical Dell SFF towers. Lateral dead space 20-50% per shelf; shelved items recessed 20-80mm behind the rail plane and yawed 1-3 degrees.
findings.fix_hint: Add a shelf-population system: 1-4 vented cantilever shelves in the middle band, each carrying 1-3 consumer-form devices occupying 0.50-0.80 of width, recessed 20-80mm, individual yaw jitter 1-3 deg, rounded-corner plastic silhouettes contrasting the steel.

findings.feature: Network band pinned to top of rack
findings.evidence: 6/6 photos: patch panel + switch occupy the top 2-4U. cavelab_2019 fan panel then UniFi switch at top; dimitrije 12-port keystone panel is U1; shawnmix 24-port panel then switch as top two units; xtremeownage ~7U contiguous network stack (finned unit, white patch panel, brush panel, white UDM) at top.
findings.fix_hint: Reserve top 2-4U for a patch panel directly above a 1U switch; patch panel is always the topmost or second unit; this band is the densest cable zone.

findings.feature: LED count budget, off-fraction, and exposure response
findings.evidence: Total lit points per rack at ambient: cavelab_2019 ~11 (6 switch port LEDs, 1 LCD glow, 3 green chassis dots, 1 blue), shawnmix ~9, dimitrije ~6 (ALL on one switch, all four OptiPlexes dark), xtremeownage ~6 visible in a bright room. Green dominates ~60%, blue ~25%, amber/red 1-2 singles. 30-60% of units show zero light. In the dim basement (shawnmix) LEDs bloom to ~3x die size with halo; in bright rooms they are 1-3px saturated points. Plan has 'LED palette' but no count budget or off-fraction, which defaults to over-lighting.
findings.fix_hint: Budget 6-12 lit points per rack TOTAL; force 30-60% of units fully dark; ratio green:blue:amber-red = 6:3:1; per-tray drive LEDs only on 1-2 active storage units; bloom radius scales inversely with scene luminance (3x die size at dim ambient, ~1x at bright).

findings.feature: Runs of identical repeated chassis
findings.evidence: Every rack contains a contiguous run of 2-4 IDENTICAL units: cavelab_2019 three identical 4U louvered-door chassis at 0.35-0.75 height (same lock, same green LED position); dimitrije four identical OptiPlex 7040 micro shelves; xtremeownage two micros side-by-side plus two 12-bay Dells; shawnmix twin rounded towers. Units differ only in seating jitter and LED state.
findings.fix_hint: Add a 'multiples' rule: pick one chassis type and instance it 2-4x contiguously; allow only +-2mm seating and LED-state variation between instances; never a rack of all-unique units.

findings.feature: Mixed-vendor face finish variety
findings.evidence: 3-6 distinct finishes per rack: xtremeownage mixes matte-black steel, white Ubiquiti plastic (patch panel + UDM), silver/grey Dell PowerVault face, and one saturated-blue LogRhythm bezel; cavelab has a WHITE frame with black gear plus a white/silver switch; dimitrije is near-monochrome but breaks it with silver Dell badges and blue cables; shawnmix has white switch + beige TrendNet PCB-tone unit in a black cabinet.
findings.fix_hint: Face-material palette per rack: base matte black + mandatory 1-2 white/silver network faces + optional one grey enterprise face + at most ONE saturated color bezel; frame color itself varies (1 of 4 unique refs is white).

findings.feature: Slack coils, dangling power catenaries, and domestic plugs
findings.evidence: cavelab_2024: excess black cable coiled in an ~6-8 turn loop (~0.12 of image width diameter) hung on the right post at ~0.55 height, plus full-height loose black power-cord catenaries down the left side. dimitrije: four chunky UK plugs face-forward in a 1U power strip at the bottom, cords draping out of frame. These read 'lived-in'; plan's restrained cabling would delete them.
findings.fix_hint: Add side dressing: one hung slack coil (6-10 turns, 120-200mm dia) on a rear post at 0.4-0.6 height; 1-3 black power-cord catenaries running down one interior side; in small cabinets, a bottom 1U outlet strip with 3-4 oversized molded plugs facing forward.

findings.feature: Top-entry cable trunk
findings.evidence: Racks are fed from above in 3/4: shawnmix has a velcro-wrapped bundle of ~10 blue cat cables dropping from ceiling joists straight into the cabinet top; xtremeownage has a teal/black cluster entering top-rear-right; cavelab_2019 has wall-draped cables descending to the top shelf. None fed from underfloor.
findings.fix_hint: Route a 8-15 cable trunk entering the rack top rear, one velcro wrap 100-200mm above entry, splaying toward the patch panel rear; never model underfloor feed.

findings.feature: Cable color economy (one dominant hue)
findings.evidence: Each rack commits to ONE saturated patch hue: dimitrije all-blue (5/5), shawnmix all-orange (~20+) with 2-3 yellow/blue accents, cavelab_2019 mixed orange/green/white but on a single switch row, xtremeownage black/green. Power cords are black in 100% of cases; fiber shows as yellow or aqua/teal singles.
findings.fix_hint: Pick one dominant cat-cable hue per rack (blue or orange preferred, >=70% of patch cables), 2-3 accent cables max (yellow/green/white), all power black, optional single aqua or yellow fiber pair near the switch SFP zone.

findings.feature: Empty cage-nut holes and visible mounting hardware
findings.evidence: In every photo >80% of square rail holes are unfilled and clearly read as a dotted vertical strip (cavelab white rack shows silver perforated strips full-height; xtremeownage black rails dotted top to bottom). Mounted units show 2-4 silver screw heads per side (dimitrije: prominent button-heads at every shelf corner, catching light); cavelab_2019 4U chassis have thumbscrew ears and handles; cavelab_2024 shows silver rail-kit ends protruding beside server faces.
findings.fix_hint: Model rail strips with full hole arrays, fill only at mount points; 2-4 raised screw heads (1-2mm proud, brighter spec than face) per unit side; thumbscrew ears on 3U+ chassis; occasional silver rail-end tabs protruding 5-10mm beside deep servers.

findings.feature: Floor interaction (casters / feet / furniture)
findings.evidence: Three distinct modes: cavelab both eras on grey swivel casters with brake levers raising the frame ~80-100mm off dark tile / quarry tile; dimitrije on round rubber feet on wood laminate; shawnmix cabinet sitting on a butcher-block counter. Baseboard cable runs and wall outlets appear within frame in cavelab_2019 and shawnmix.
findings.fix_hint: Give the rack casters (60-80mm dia, one brake lever visible) OR four cylindrical feet; raise bottom rail 60-100mm; allow one cable to spill toward a baseboard outlet at floor level.

findings.feature: Seating jitter parameterization (rackmount vs shelved)
findings.evidence: Plan includes seating jitter but refs split it in two regimes: screwed rackmount faces vary only 0-10mm in depth and essentially zero yaw (cavelab_2019 triplet is flush within ~1 face-pixel), while shelf-sitting gear varies 20-80mm recess with visible 1-3 deg yaw (cavelab_2024 HP boxes, shawnmix towers not parallel). A single global jitter value would either over-wobble the bolted units or under-wobble the shelved ones.
findings.fix_hint: Two jitter classes: bolted units depth +-5mm, yaw <0.5 deg; shelved units recess 20-80mm, yaw 1-3 deg, lateral offset up to 15% of shelf width.

findings.feature: Top-of-rack surface as storage shelf
findings.evidence: cavelab_2019 top hosts a white consumer router, a large glossy-black Synology tower NAS, and draped cable slack; shawnmix cabinet top receives the ceiling trunk across its vent; xtremeownage top rail carries a small white device and cable cluster. Only the small dimitrije cabinet keeps its (decorative pinwheel-cutout) top clear.
findings.fix_hint: Place 1-3 consumer objects on the top surface (router, tower NAS, cable slack pile), offset from centerline, off the fan cutout; leave clear only on small desktop cabinets.

findings.feature: Dust placement law
findings.evidence: Plan has micro-roughness/dust but the refs localize it: xtremeownage shows grey speckle concentrated on upward-facing horizontal edges - the base plinth (heaviest, with lint specks), the tops of the two wide black rails at ~0.35 and ~0.55 height - while vertical faces stay clean; cavelab_2024 shelves show faint grey haze on their lips. Uniform dust would read wrong.
findings.fix_hint: Weight dust by face normal: horizontal-up surfaces get 5-10x the speckle density of vertical faces, doubling again on the bottom plinth; add sparse lint specks (0.5-2mm) at the base only; mesh areas get faint uniform haze.

findings.feature: UPS LCD panel glow
findings.evidence: The only area-emissive element in any rack is a single UPS LCD near the bottom: cavelab_2019 PowerWalker LCD glows blue-white and blooms in flash exposure; shawnmix CyberPower LCD dark-blue with a lit round blue power button. Exactly one LCD per rack, always on the UPS.
findings.fix_hint: One 40x20mm-class LCD on the bottom UPS, blue-white emission readable at all exposures, plus one 8-10mm round lit power button; no other area emitters.

findings.feature: Blanking-panel scarcity and panel variety
findings.evidence: Gaps are mostly left OPEN: only cavelab_2019 has a single branded 1U blank (NavePoint) and xtremeownage one 1U blank + one 1U brush panel out of ~25U; dimitrije uses mesh inserts beside the micros. No rack is fully blanked - showroom racks always are.
findings.fix_hint: At most 1 blank + 1 brush panel per rack; leave remaining gaps as open rails; brand one blank with a small centered white logo.

findings.feature: Decorative fan cutouts and vent pattern variety
findings.evidence: Vent geometry is varied and sometimes ornamental: dimitrije top panel has a spiral pinwheel cutout; cavelab_2019 top unit has three circular spiral-grille fan cutouts with copper blades visible behind; chassis fronts mix louvers (cavelab 4U doors), diamond mesh (OptiPlex right halves), and round-hole perforation.
findings.fix_hint: Use at least 3 distinct perforation patterns across the rack (louver, diamond mesh, round hole) and one decorative spiral fan cutout on a top or side panel with fan blades 5-10mm behind.

findings.feature: Personal / whimsical object
findings.evidence: shawnmix has a miniature model house with a green roof sitting ON a tower unit inside the rack at ~0.55 height; cavelab_2019 has a cocktail tin sign on the adjacent wall; xtremeownage sits under a pink 'EMMA' wall decal. One human object per scene, always off the gear centerline.
findings.fix_hint: Place exactly one small non-tech object (toy, figurine, sign) either on an interior shelf edge or adjacent wall, offset from center, 40-80mm scale.

findings.feature: Ambient environment context (corner, clutter, walls)
findings.evidence: All racks are pressed into corners or against walls within ~150mm: white drywall + tile (cavelab_2019), whitewashed rough plaster + brick-red quarry tile (cavelab_2024), foil insulation + parged concrete basement (shawnmix), cream wall + oak laminate closet (xtremeownage). Within 1m: desks with tools, wall-warts in outlets, shelving with bins, a wall monitor.
findings.fix_hint: Stage the rack <=150mm from a wall/corner; choose one domestic floor (tile, quarry tile, or wood laminate); add 2-3 clutter props within 1m and one wall-mounted item (outlet with wall-wart, vent, or sign).

findings.feature: Rear cable-side geometry
findings.evidence: All six photos are front or three-quarter-front; no reference shows the rear cable field, PDU mounting, or rear rail dress. Any rear-face rules would be invented.
findings.fix_hint: Source 2-3 rear-view homelab photos before speccing the rear; until then keep camera coverage front-hemisphere only.

grades.why: The catalog covers all requested families (fill, cable dress, LED landscape, dust, vendor mix, top-of-rack, floor, environment) with per-photo citations and ratio estimates, but it is built from only 4 unique racks, front views only, mixed exposures, and no macro detail - rear geometry, wear-around-touchpoints, and LED behavior under controlled exposure are interpolated, not measured.

The current plan (LED palette, seating jitter, micro-roughness/dust, restrained cabling) covers 4 of ~11 rule families the references demand, and one of the four - 'restrained cabling' - is directionally WRONG: every owned rack shows expressive front-crossing patch arcs, slack coils, and dangling power catenaries. Missing entirely: fill/gap map, bottom-heavy ordering, shelf-mounted consumer gear, identical-unit runs, mixed-vendor faces, top-of-rack objects, floor interaction, hardware/empty-hole truth.

The plan contains no fill-distribution model at all; an unparameterized build defaults to uniform, fully-populated faces, which contradicts the single most consistent reference truth - 60-85% fill, one 2-5U upper-middle void, network pinned top, heavy contiguous bottom block, shelves of undersized consumer gear in between. Until a fill map exists this aspect cannot score above 2.

notes: Reference-quality caveats: the six files reduce to FOUR unique racks - cavelab_2019 and cavelab_2024 are the same white NavePoint-era frame five years apart (valuable as a time-lapse of ownership: same frame, completely re-dressed), and the two xtremeownage webp files are one StarTech rack (the second is a tighter annotated crop confirming R730XD/R720XD as the bottom pair). All views are front or three-quarter front; there is zero rear-face ground truth, so rear cable-field rules cannot be written yet. Exposures vary (direct flash in cavelab_2019 vs dim ambient in shawnmix vs bright room in xtremeownage), which is actually useful - it brackets the LED-bloom-vs-exposure rule - but absolute LED luminance is not recoverable. No absolute dimensions are available; all numbers in findings are pixel-ratio estimates against known anchors (1U pitch, OptiPlex micro width 182mm, BS1363 plug width 50mm). All grades reflect the plan/spec state, not a render, since no render frames exist for this face.

findings.feature: Panel FALSE-TELL calibration mechanics (desktop 10b/10c/10d/10e)
findings.evidence: Rack docs carry only the name: RACK-BUILD-PLAN sec 8 'real-photo pool now includes real datacenter/homelab rack photos of OTHER gear' and RACK-LOOP Part 6 'driver reads verdicts against control calibration'. The desktop burned 15 panel loops learning three mechanisms none of which are written into the rack protocol: (a) clean-product FALSE-TELL — a genuine Apple press photo scored 4/5 render judged ALONE, 5/5 in loop 16; REAL control rate swung 0.03-0.45; (b) mixed-batch cross-contamination (10c) — agents pattern-matched 'same foam as img_02/08/09/10/12' onto REAL photos; (c) the vote-vs-tell line-141/142 criterion conflict that only the grader can rule (10e). The rack triples the 10c hazard: 3 IDENTICAL linked-dupe RM44 nodes in every frame are same-asset clustering bait. And the false-tell POLARITY inverts for the rack: it is HANDLED hardware (dust/wear honest per BUILD-PLAN sec 4), so the desktop clean-product baseline does not apply — the rack needs its own pre-run baseline of what real homelab photos score.
findings.fix_hint: Add a panel-protocol block to RACK-LOOP Part 6 citing PHOTOREAL-SHIFT-REPORT 10b-10e by section: (1) BEFORE any gate loop, panel the real rack-photo pool alone and record the REAL render-call baseline; (2) pass bar = MINE within the REAL band, never absolute-clean; (3) an isolation-mode (single image per agent) run is the tiebreak when batch clustering is suspected — mandatory given 3 identical nodes; (4) two consecutive readings before believing any delta (GEOMETRY-TEN step 8); (5) a criterion-internal-conflict is an ESCALATE class, not an iterate class.

findings.feature: Flip-flop guard (two-reference re-measure before re-changing a value)
findings.evidence: GEOMETRY-TEN Part 1 rule 5 is explicit: a value already changed once on visual evidence moves again ONLY after fresh measurement across >=2 independent references written to MEASUREMENTS first (scar: weave pitch 4.6->1.5->'audit says 5mm'). RACK-LOOP invariants protect PINS (invariant 1) but say nothing about re-changing loop-owned values. Live flip-flop candidates already exist in the rack docs: lock cylinder 'APPROX row · re-measure', mesh open fraction 'LOW-CONF 0.4-0.5 refine at part wave', ups_black 'LOW-CONF pin (440px photo)', and O_rack has ALREADY flipped once (+6 -> 0.0 in the autopsy).
findings.fix_hint: Insert as RACK-LOOP invariant 7, verbatim from GEOMETRY-TEN rule 5, and tag the four LOW-CONF/APPROX values above as explicitly under the guard in the WORK QUEUE lines that touch them.

findings.feature: Camera physics: DOF-per-shot-class table + true-macro detail rule (L18)
findings.evidence: PHOTOREAL-SHIFT sec 4: f5.6 detail / f11 pair / f16 wide, plus the L18 autopsy — the delivered 'detail' was silently a CROP of the sharp f16 q34 frame for 15 loops and the panel correctly named 'uniform sharp focus at macro' the whole time. The rack portraits (RACK-LOOP Part 6, BUILD-PLAN sec 8) list TWO macro-class shots (node-face detail, empty-bay depth detail) plus the raking acceptance tile and the trio, with zero f-stop or aim specification anywhere in the rack docs.
findings.fix_hint: Add the camera table to RACK-LOOP Part 6: detail shots = shot-class 'detail', f5.6, FEATURE-aimed (mesh-hole field for the node, rail hole row for the empty bay), never a crop of a wider frame; trio/full-rack f11-f16. Also transfer the line-140 alternation lesson: detail subjects rotate across parts, not always the same face.

findings.feature: Post chain transfer (grain/CA/bloom/vignette/roll) + gate-is-pre-post ordering
findings.evidence: PHOTOREAL-SHIFT sec 6: post_chain.py (roll 0.3deg · CA ±0.18% · bloom thr 0.88 · vignette 0.80 · grain sigma 0.008), applied AFTER the gated raw, post-delta measured < 0.6 L, 'post never rescues a failing pin'. Zero occurrences of post/grain/CA/vignette/roll in RACK-LOOP, RACK-BUILD-PLAN, RACK-NOTES, or rack_verify.py; rack_verify's docstring does not state whether it judges pre- or post-chain frames, so the Opus loop could legally gate post-processed frames.
findings.fix_hint: One line in rack_verify.py header: 'gates the PRE-POST raw only'; one bullet in RACK-LOOP Part 6: inherit post_chain.py values verbatim for the photoreal wave, re-measure post-delta on the rack patches (<0.6 L) once, before the panel.

findings.feature: Scale-trio shared-rig re-verification mechanics (per-object-class offsets)
findings.evidence: The PRINCIPLE is well transferred (RACK-LOOP invariant 4, BUILD-PLAN secs 1/6, risk register MED row, NOTES resolution 'one rig, per-object-class offsets'). The MECHANICS are absent: rack_verify.py has a single global O_RACK scalar, PINS contains only the 4 rack materials (no studio_alu/spark_champ/spark_top/spark_foam), SHOTS has no trio entry, and there is no written command sequence for re-checking the desktop pins from this worktree while invariant 2 declares the desktop files read-only. As written, invariant 4's 're-checks the desktop pins' is unexecutable by the loop operator.
findings.fix_hint: Extend rack_verify SHOTS entries to 4-tuples (pin, box, note, offset) so one trio frame gates rack patches at 0.0 and desktop patches at -12 simultaneously; add the desktop re-check recipe (render read-only build_scene shots, run the desktop rig_patches gate, paste both outputs in NOTES) as a numbered procedure under invariant 4.

findings.feature: Firefly clamp
findings.evidence: Desktop needed 'firefly clamp + clean deliverables' at panel loop 13 on scenes far simpler than the rack. The rack scene is a firefly generator: ~7,500 real cut holes x3 node doors (25,392 tris per bake-off tile), thin 0.4-0.5mm webs, emissive LEDs, an interior fill light inside each node, and semi-bright zinc cage nuts. No clamp/denoiser/sample spec appears in any rack doc or in the build_rack defaults quoted in RACK-LOOP invariant 4.
findings.fix_hint: Pin the desktop Cycles clamp-indirect value into build_rack.py render defaults and list it beside key/rim/fill in invariant 4 so changing it is also a LIGHTING-class commit.

findings.feature: Mesh-pitch acceptance tool lives in ephemeral scratchpad, not the repo
findings.evidence: Part 1 acceptance (c) — the only numeric geometry check on the hero part — reads 'mesh pitch measures 2.87/2.59 +-5% via the FFT autocorr (scratchpad rm44 script method)'. Scratchpads are session-scoped; the Opus loop operator inherits a acceptance criterion whose measuring instrument does not exist in the worktree. The desktop equivalents (rig_patches.py, measure scripts) are committed files.
findings.fix_hint: Commit the FFT autocorrelation as render/mesh_pitch_verify.py (or a --pitch mode on rack_verify.py taking px/mm + crop box) BEFORE Part 1 starts; reference it by repo path in the acceptance line.

findings.feature: Autopsy-on-overturned-value protocol (codified, not just practiced)
findings.evidence: The rack PRACTICES it superbly once (O_rack +6 -> 0.0 autopsy in RACK-NOTES + in-file note in rack_verify.py lines 25-29 — the oracle caught the driver). But GEOMETRY-TEN rule 4 makes it a standing RULE ('any overturned pin gets an AUTOPSY line in MEASUREMENTS.md: old value, why wrong, what replaced it'); RACK-LOOP's invariants and its NOTES ENTRY TEMPLATE have no autopsy requirement or field, so an Opus-side overturn (e.g. the APPROX lock position) can be silently replaced.
findings.fix_hint: Add rule-4 verbatim to RACK-LOOP invariants and an optional 'Autopsy:' line to the NOTES ENTRY TEMPLATE.

findings.feature: Vision-audit arbitration + ortho-frame measurement protocol
findings.evidence: GEOMETRY-AUDIT's method is scar tissue: agent findings 'cross-checked against the frozen pins and my own direct frame reads... every conflict flagged rather than blindly actioned'; two desktop pins were corrupted by perspective (led_x 87.70 measured through corner foreshortening; the dimensional unit's 3/10 proportion overruled — spec-exact 0.482 vs 0.481). A 1991mm-tall cabinet suffers vertical perspective compression far worse than a 95mm desktop, and RACK-NOTES 5a's frame verdict ('proportions read as a true 42U... 2.65:1') is an eyeball claim with no ortho render measurement logged — the risk register's own 'measure.py verify on the render' was not executed before freezing the frame.
findings.fix_hint: Add to RACK-LOOP: all proportion/position/pitch claims on the cabinet are measured on ortho/long-lens frames only; any future multi-agent geometry audit is arbitrated against MEASUREMENTS pins before actioning; retro-verify the frozen frame's rail pitch (44.45mm/U ±1%) and 2.65:1 ratio on an ortho front as a zero-cost first loop item.

findings.feature: Per-item acceptance checks with numeric ratios (all parts, not just 1-2)
findings.evidence: Coverage is front-loaded: Part 1 has real numerics (P=2.87/R=2.59 ±5% FFT; rack_verify PASS), Part 2 has 'silhouette vs CAD dims ±1%'. Parts 3-6 degrade to qualitative reads: 'reads recessed', 'reads correct in a 3-unit stack', 'no two adjacent units read identical', 'square holes crisply', 'openings-with-interior'. GEOMETRY-TEN gives EVERY queue item evidence -> change -> numeric acceptance (within 5%/10%/1%/3%).
findings.fix_hint: Add numbers to parts 3-6: U-gap 0.79mm visible in the stack render (measured, not 'reads'); assembly rail-pitch FFT on the full front ±1%; LED-state census matches the {off,green,amber} palette draw; seating jitter measured <=0.4deg on silhouette edges; empty-bay hole rows counted 3/U over U22-42.

findings.feature: Checkpoint/deliverable discipline (16-attachment folder, reshoot cadence)
findings.evidence: GEOMETRY-TEN rule 9 + step 7: checkpoint folder <=16 attachments, rebuilt by _checkpoint_export.py, full 4K reshoot + collages every ~5 commits or when a queue empties; PHOTOREAL-SHIFT sec 11 shows the delivered form. The rack docs specify per-iteration evidence renders and commits but no deliverable cadence, no checkpoint folder, no collage/contact-sheet step anywhere.
findings.fix_hint: Append a 'Reporting' section to RACK-LOOP mirroring GEOMETRY-TEN Part 5: rack checkpoint folder (<=16), rebuilt at each part-freeze and at gates 6/9/10.

findings.feature: Authority hierarchy + verify-vs-eyeball conflict rule
findings.evidence: GEOMETRY-TEN rule 1 states the chain (measurement > document > panel > own eye); PHOTOREAL-SHIFT held it 'throughout'. RACK-LOOP step 4 requires 'exit 0 AND Read the image' but never says which wins when they disagree (oracle PASS + eyeball FAIL, or the reverse) — the exact split the O_rack autopsy shows will happen (eyeball said +6, oracle said 0).
findings.fix_hint: Restate the hierarchy as invariant 0 and one sentence in step 4: oracle-vs-eye disagreement is logged and escalated, never resolved by retuning either side.

findings.feature: Foam-style geometry caching for the mesh door array
findings.evidence: Named twice ('cached like foam3d' in Part 1 queue; 'foam3d-style cache' in risk register) — the lesson's existence transferred. But GEOMETRY-TEN rule 8's mechanics did not: cache location (render/cache/), key = geometry params so geometry changes auto-rekey while material-only changes reuse. Unstated for the rack, an Opus operator could hand-manage staleness on a 25k-tri x3 boolean product.
findings.fix_hint: One line in Part 1: cache under render/cache/ keyed on hash(P, R, shrink, THICK, border, door dims); material commits must not touch the key.

findings.feature: Technique-class bake-off doctrine (switch-and-log)
findings.evidence: Best transfer in the set: RACK-LOOP invariant 3 locks the class, BUILD-PLAN sec 5 mandates a two-technique bake-off with the grader line-149 precedent cited, and RACK-NOTES 5b actually RAN it (A real-cut vs B alpha-mask, raking + graze, failure documented in bake-B-raking.png) before the loop started — the Spark heightfield scar applied proactively rather than after 8 wasted loops.
findings.fix_hint: None · keep the logged-bake-off requirement exactly as written.

findings.feature: Anti-drift / imperfection-is-seasoning calibration
findings.evidence: BUILD-PLAN sec 4 carries the L5 grunge-autopsy scar verbatim and correctly re-derives its polarity for the new subject: 'added imperfection is the tell on CLEAN product · but a homelab rack is HANDLED hardware · light wear/dust is honest here', with the discipline that every variance knob traces to something reference-visible.
findings.fix_hint: None · already correctly transferred and re-polarized.

findings.feature: Escalation boundaries (stop triggers)
findings.evidence: RACK-LOOP's ESCALATE section is explicit and mostly complete: pin doubt, judgment calls the checklist can't answer, 3-consecutive-fail technique exhaustion, rig changes, desktop files, panel gate, trio composition — all driver-owned. Two desktop scar classes are absent: the 10e 'pass criterion is internally conflicted' escalation class, and capacity-cap handling (loop 15 ran partial on the weekly subagent cap with no protocol for a truncated panel).
findings.fix_hint: Add two triggers: 'the gate's own pass criterion conflicts with itself or with a FALSE-TELL ruling' and 'a panel/agent run truncates on capacity — report partial, never extrapolate'.

grades.why: Strong skeleton — invariants, one-bounded-change iterations, per-part freeze, work queue, notes template, 3-fail stop. But a machinist following only RACK-LOOP would re-commit four desktop injuries: no flip-flop guard, no post-chain/gate ordering, no camera DOF spec, no checkpoint cadence, and no authority-hierarchy statement for oracle-vs-eye conflicts.

rack_verify.py is honest and self-documenting (clip gate, 4 pins with per-pin tolerances, the O_rack autopsy in-file) but thin: one shot has patch boxes, no trio/multi-offset support despite invariant 4 requiring desktop re-checks, no pre-post declaration, and the ONLY numeric geometry acceptance (mesh-pitch FFT) references an uncommitted scratchpad script. The oracle covers tone; geometry verification is mostly promissory.

The cheap-to-name lessons transferred well (technique bake-off actually pre-run, anti-drift re-polarized, tone-senior, clip gate, notes discipline, cache and control-calibration by name). The expensive ones did not: the 15-loop panel calibration mechanics (10b-10e), the L18 fake-detail camera scar, the post chain, and the flip-flop guard are absent — precisely the lessons that cost the desktop the most to learn.

The best-transferred aspect: seven explicit driver-owned stop triggers, pins-never-move with the laundering autopsy cited as law, desktops declared closed, 3-fail exhaustion rule. Docked for the missing criterion-conflict class (the exact deadlock 10e proved needs grader ruling) and no verify-vs-eyeball arbitration rule inside the loop step itself.

notes: Process audit, documents only — no render frames existed to compare, so all statuses describe protocol presence, not geometry. Status vocabulary mapped: correct = lesson fully transferred; missing = absent from all four rack docs; wrong_shape = present by name but mechanics understated; wrong_proportion = coverage uneven across the work queue. Caveats: (1) referenced artifacts not provided — MEASUREMENTS.md RACK section, D-ARCH.md, _rack_bakeoff.py, PANEL-LOG.md, geometry-audit-raw.json — so claims about their internal content are unverifiable; the fill map and homelab-gestalt pool are assumed to exist as described. (2) RACK-BUILD-PLAN section 0 tone table and the bake-off evidence PNGs were taken at face value. (3) The two strongest single artifacts in the rack set are the pre-run bake-off (RACK-NOTES 5b) and the O_rack autopsy where the oracle overturned the driver's own +6 claim — that autopsy is the best evidence the pin discipline is genuinely live, which is why its absence as a codified RULE (vs a practiced instance) is graded partial rather than missing. (4) Highest-leverage single insertion: the panel FALSE-TELL block (finding 1) — the rack's 3 identical linked-dupe nodes make the 10c batch-clustering failure mode strictly worse than the desktop case that already consumed 15 loops."
